text: The vision of Isaias the son of Amos, which he saw concerning Juda and Jerusalem in the days of Ozias, Joathan, Achaz, and Ezechias, kings of Juda.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, O ye heavens, and give ear, O earth, for the Lord hath spoken. I have brought up children, and exalted them: but they have despised me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The ox knoweth his owner, and the ass his master’s crib: but Israel hath not known me, and my people hath not understood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to the sinful nation, a people laden with iniquity, a wicked seed, ungracious children: they have forsaken the Lord, they have blasphemed the Holy One of Israel, they are gone away backwards.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For what shall I strike you any more, you that increase transgression? the whole head is sick, and the whole heart is sad.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From the sole of the foot unto the top of the head, there is no soundness therein: wounds and bruises and swelling sores: they are not bound up, nor dressed, nor fomented with oil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your land is desolate, your cities are burnt with fire: your country strangers devour before your face, and it shall be desolate as when wasted by enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the daughter of Sion shall be left as a covert in a vineyard, and as a lodge in a garden of cucumbers, and as a city that is laid waste.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Except the Lord of hosts had left us seed, we had been as Sodom, and we should have been like to Gomorrha.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear the word of the Lord, ye rulers of Sodom, give ear to the law of our God, ye people of Gomorrha.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To what purpose do you offer me the multitude of your victims, saith the Lord? I am full, I desire not holocausts of rams, and fat of fatlings, and blood of calves, and lambs, and buck goats.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When you came to appear before me, who required these things at your hands, that you should walk in my courts?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Offer sacrifice no more in vain: incense is an abomination to me. The new moons, and the sabbaths and other festivals I will not abide, your assemblies are wicked.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My soul hateth your new moons, and your solemnities: they are become troublesome to me, I am weary of bearing them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when you stretch forth your hands, I will turn away my eyes from you: and when you multiply prayer, I will not hear: for your hands are full of blood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wash yourselves, be clean, take away the evil of your devices from my eyes, cease to do perversely,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Learn to do well: seek judgment, relieve the oppressed, judge for the fatherless, defend the widow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And then come, and accuse me, saith the Lord: if your sins be as scarlet, they shall be made as white as snow: and if they be red as crimson, they shall be white as wool.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you be willing, and will hearken to me, you shall eat the good things of the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you will not, and will provoke me to wrath: the sword shall devour you because the mouth of the Lord hath spoken it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How is the faithful city, that was full of judgment, become a harlot? justice dwelt in it, but now murderers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy silver is turned into dross: thy wine is mingled with water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy princes are faithless, companions of thieves: they all love bribes, they run after rewards. They judge not for the fatherless: and the widow’s cause cometh not in to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore saith the Lord the God of hosts, the mighty one of Israel: Ah! I will comfort myself over my adversaries: and I will be revenged of my enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will turn my hand to thee, and I will clean purge away thy dross, and I will take away all thy tin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will restore thy judges as they were before, and thy counsellors as of old. After this thou shalt be called the city of the just, a faithful city.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sion shall be redeemed in judgment, and they shall bring her back in justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall destroy the wicked, and the sinners together: and they that have forsaken the Lord, shall be consumed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they shall be confounded for the idols, to which they have sacrificed: and you shall be ashamed of the gardens which you have chosen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When you shall be as an oak with the leaves falling off, and as a garden without water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And your strength shall be as the ashes of tow, and your work as a spark: and both shall burn together, and there shall be none to quench it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The word that Isaias the son of Amos saw, concerning Juda and Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the last days the mountain of the house of the Lord shall be prepared on the top of mountains, and it shall be exalted above the hills, and all nations shall flow unto it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And many people shall go, and say: Come and let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, and to the house of the God of Jacob, and he will teach us his ways, and we will walk in his paths: for the law shall come forth from Sion, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall judge the Gentiles, and rebuke many people: and they shall turn their swords into ploughshares, and their spears into sickles: nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they be exercised any more to war.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O house of Jacob, come ye, and let us walk in the light of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thou hast cast off thy people, the house of Jacob: because they are filled as in times past, and have had soothsayers as the Philistines, and have adhered to strange children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their land is filled with silver and gold: and there is no end of their treasures.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their land is filled with horses: and their chariots are innumerable. Their land also is full of idols: they have adored the work of their own hands, which their own fingers have made.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And man hath bowed himself down, and man hath been debased: therefore forgive them not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Enter thou into the rock, and hide thee in the pit from the face of the fear of the Lord, and from the glory of his majesty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The lofty eyes of man are humbled, and the haughtiness of men shall be made to stoop: and the Lord alone shall be exalted in that day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because the day of the Lord of hosts shall be upon every one that is proud and highminded, and upon every one that is arrogant, and he shall be humbled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And upon all the tall and lofty cedars of Libanus, and upon all the oaks of Basan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And upon all the high mountains and upon all the elevated hills.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And upon every high tower, and every fenced wall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And upon all the ships of Tharsis, and upon all that is fair to behold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the loftiness of men shall be bowed down, and the haughtiness of men shall be humbled, and the Lord alone shall be exalted in that day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And idols shall be utterly destroyed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall go into the holes of rocks, and into the caves of the earth from the face of the fear of the Lord, and from the glory of his majesty, when he shall rise up to strike the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day a man shall cast away his idols of silver, and his idols of gold, which he had made for himself to adore, moles and bats.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall go into the clefts of rocks, and into the holes of stones from the face of the fear of the Lord, and from the glory of his majesty, when he shall rise up to strike the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cease ye therefore from the man, whose breath is in his nostrils, for he is reputed high.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For behold the sovereign Lord of hosts shall take away from Jerusalem, and from Juda the valiant and the strong, the whole strength of bread, and the whole strength of water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The strong man, and the man of war, the judge, and the prophet and the cunning man, and the ancient.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The captain over fifty, and the honourable in countenance, and the counsellor, and the architect, and the skilful in eloquent speech.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will give children to be their princes, and the effeminate shall rule over them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people shall rush one upon another, and every man against his neighbour: the child shall make a tumult against the ancient, and the base against the honourable.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For a man shall take hold of his brother, one of the house of his father, saying: Thou hast a garment, be thou our ruler, and let this ruin be under thy hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day he shall answer, saying: I am no healer, and in my house there is no bread, nor clothing: make me not ruler of the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For Jerusalem is ruined, and Juda is fallen: because their tongue, and their devices are against the Lord, to provoke the eyes of his majesty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The shew of their countenance hath answered them: and they have proclaimed abroad their sin as Sodom, and they have not hid it: woe to their souls, for evils are rendered to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say to the just man that it is well, for he shall eat the fruit of his doings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to the wicked unto evil: for the reward of his hands shall be given him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As for my people, their oppressors have stripped them, and women have ruled over them. O my people, they that call thee blessed, the same deceive thee, and destroy the way of thy steps.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord standeth up to judge, and he standeth to judge the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will enter into judgment with the ancients of his people, and its princes: for you have devoured the vineyard, and the spoil of the poor is in your house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why do you consume my people, and grind the faces of the poor? saith the Lord the God of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said: Because the daughters of Sion are haughty, and have walked with stretched out necks, and wanton glances of their eyes, and made a noise as they walked with their feet and moved in a set pace:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will make bald the crown of the head of the daughters of Sion, and the Lord will discover their hair.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day the Lord will take away the ornaments of shoes, and little moons,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And chains and necklaces, and bracelets, and bonnets,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And bodkins, and ornaments of the legs, and tablets, and sweet balls, and earrings,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And rings, and jewels hanging on the forehead,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And changes of apparel, and short cloaks, and fine linen, and crisping pins,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And lookingglasses, and lawns, and headbands, and fine veils.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And instead of a sweet smell there shall be stench, and instead of a girdle, a cord, and instead of curled hair, baldness, and instead of a stomacher, haircloth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy fairest men also shall fall by the sword, and thy valiant ones in battle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And her gates shall lament and mourn, and she shall sit desolate on the ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in that day seven women shall take hold of one man, saying: We will eat our own bread, and wear our own apparel: only let us be called by thy name, take away our reproach.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day the bud of the Lord shall be in magnificence and glory, and the fruit of the earth shall be high, and a great joy to them that shall have escaped of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass, that every one that shall be left in Sion, and that shall remain in Jerusalem, shall be called holy, every one that is written in life in Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the Lord shall wash away the filth of the daughters of Sion, and shall wash away the blood of Jerusalem out of the midst thereof, by the spirit of judgment, and by the spirit of burning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord will create upon every place of mount Sion, and where he is called upon, a cloud by day, and a smoke and the brightness of a flaming fire in the night: for over all the glory shall be a protection.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be a tabernacle for a shade in the daytime from the heat, and for a security and covert from the whirlwind, and from rain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will sing to my beloved the canticle of my cousin concerning his vineyard. My beloved had a vineyard on a hill in a fruitful place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he fenced it in, and picked the stones out of it, and planted it with the choicest vines, and built a tower in the midst thereof, and set up a winepress therein: and he looked that it should bring forth grapes, and it brought forth wild grapes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now, O ye inhabitants of Jerusalem, and ye men of Juda, judge between me and my vineyard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What is there that I ought to do more to my vineyard, that I have not done to it? was it that I looked that it should bring forth grapes, and it hath brought forth wild grapes?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now I will shew you what I will do to my vineyard. I will take away the hedge thereof, and it shall be wasted: I will break down the wall thereof, and it shall be trodden down.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make it desolate: it shall not be pruned, and it shall not be digged: but briers and thorns shall come up: and I will command the clouds to rain no rain upon it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the vineyard of the Lord of hosts is the house of Israel: and the man of Juda, his pleasant plant: and I looked that he should do judgment, and behold iniquity: and do justice, and behold a cry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you that join house to house and lay field to field, even to the end of the place: shall you alone dwell in the midst of the earth?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These things are in my ears, saith the Lord of hosts: Unless many great and fair houses shall become desolate, without an inhabitant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For ten acres of vineyard shall yield one little measure, and thirty bushels of seed shall yield three bushels.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you that rise up early in the morning to follow drunkenness, and to drink till the evening, to be inflamed with wine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The harp, and the lyre, and, the timbrel and the pipe, and wine are in your feasts: and the work of the Lord you regard not, nor do you consider the works of his hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore is my people led away captive, because they had not knowledge, and their nobles have perished with famine, and their multitude were dried up with thirst.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore hath hell enlarged her soul, and opened her mouth without any bounds, and their strong ones, and their people, and their high and glorious ones shall go down into it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And man shall be brought down, and man shall be humbled, and the eyes of the lofty shall be brought low.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord of hosts shall be exalted in judgment, and the holy God shall be sanctified in justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the lambs shall feed according to their order, and strangers shall eat the deserts turned into fruitfulness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you that draw iniquity with cords of vanity, and sin as the rope of a cart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That say: Let him make haste, and let his work come quickly, that we may see it: and let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel come, that we may know it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you that call evil good, and good evil: that put darkness for light, and light for darkness: that put bitter for sweet, and sweet for bitter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you that are wise in your own eyes, and prudent in your own conceits.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you that are mighty to drink wine, and stout men at drunkenness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That justify the wicked for gifts, and take away the justice of the just from him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore as the tongue of the fire devoureth the stubble, and the heat of the flame consumeth it: so shall their root be as ashes, and their bud shall go up as dust: for they have cast away the law of the Lord of hosts, and have blasphemed the word of the Holy One of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore is the wrath of the Lord kindled against his people, and he hath stretched out his hand upon them, and struck them: and the mountains were troubled, and their carcasses became as dung in the midst of the streets. For after this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he will lift up a sign to the nations afar off, and will whistle to them from the ends of the earth: and behold they shall come with speed swiftly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is none that shall faint, nor labour among them: they shall not slumber nor sleep, neither shall the girdle of their loins be loosed, nor the latchet of their shoes be broken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their arrows are sharp, and all their bows are bent. The hoofs of their horses shall be like the flint, and their wheels like the violence of a tempest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their roaring like that of a lion, they shall roar like young lions: yea they shall roar, and take hold of the prey, and they shall keep fast hold of it, and there shall be none to deliver it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall make a noise against them that day, like the roaring of the sea; we shall look towards the land, and behold darkness of tribulation, and the light is darkened with the mist thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the year that king Ozias died, I saw the Lord sitting upon a throne high and elevated: and his train filled the temple.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Upon it stood the seraphims: the one had six wings, and the other had six wings: with two they covered his face, and with two they covered his feet, and with two they flew.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they cried one to another, and said: Holy, holy, holy, the Lord God of hosts, all the earth is full of his glory,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the lintels of the doors were moved at the voice of him that cried, and the house was filled with smoke.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I said: Woe is me, because I have held my peace; because I am a man of unclean lips, and I dwell in the midst of a people that hath unclean lips, and I have seen with my eyes the King the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And one of the seraphims flew to me, and in his hand was a live coal, which he had taken with the tongs off the altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he touched my mouth, and said: Behold this hath touched thy lips, and thy iniquities shall be taken away, and thy sin shall be cleansed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I heard the voice of the Lord, saying: Whom shall I send? and who shall go for us? And I said: Lo, here am I, send me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Go, and thou shalt say to this people: Hearing, hear, and understand not: and see the vision, and know it not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blind the heart of this people, and make their ears heavy, and shut their eyes: lest they see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and be converted and I heal them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I said: How long, O Lord? And he said: Until the cities be wasted without inhabitant, and the houses without man, and the land shall be left desolate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord shall remove men far away, and she shall be multiplied that was left in the midst of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be still a tithing therein, and she shall turn, and shall be made a show as a turpentine tree, and as an oak that spreadeth its branches: that which shall stand therein, shall be a holy seed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass in the days of Achaz the son of Joathan, the son of Ozias, king of Juda, that Rasin king of Syria and Phacee the son of Romelia king of Israel, came up to Jerusalem, to fight against it: but they could not prevail over it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they told the house of David, saying: Syria hath rested upon Ephraim, and his heart was moved, and the heart of his people, as the trees of the woods are moved with the wind.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Isaias: Go forth to meet Achaz, thou and Jasub thy son that is left, to the conduit of the upper pool in the way of the fuller’s field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt say to him: See thou be quiet: fear not, and let not thy heart be afraid of the two tails of these firebrands, smoking with the wrath of the fury of Rasin king of Syria, and of the son of Romelia.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because Syria hath taken counsel against thee, unto the evil of Ephraim and the son of Romelia, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let us go up to Juda, and rouse it up, and draw it away to us, and make the son of Tabeel king in the midst thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord God: It shall not stand, and this shall not be.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the head of Syria is Damascus, and the head of Damascus is Rasin: and within threescore and five years, Ephraim shall cease to be a people:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the head of Ephraim is Samaria, and the head of Samaria is the son of Romelia. If you will not believe, you shall not continue.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke again to Achaz, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ask thee a sign of the Lord thy God, either unto the depth of hell, or unto the height above.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Achaz said: I will not ask, and I will not tempt the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Hear ye therefore, O house of David: Is it a small thing for you to be grievous to men, that you are grievous to my God also?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign. Behold a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son and his name shall be called Emmanuel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall eat butter and honey, that he may know to refuse the evil, and to choose the good.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For before the child know to refuse the evil and to choose the good, the land which thou abhorrest shall be forsaken of the face of her two kings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord shall bring upon thee, and upon thy people, and upon the house of thy father, days that have not come since the time of the separation of Ephraim from Juda with the king of the Assyrians.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the Lord shall hiss for the fly, that is in the uttermost parts of the rivers of Egypt, and for the bee that is in the land of Assyria.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall come, and shall all of them rest in the torrents of the valleys, and in the holes of the rocks, and upon all places set with shrubs, and in all hollow places.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day the Lord shall shave with a razor that is hired by them that are beyond the river, by the king of the Assyrians, the head and the hairs of the feet, and the whole beard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that a man shall nourish a young cow, and two sheep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for the abundance of milk he shall eat butter: for butter and honey shall every one eat that shall be left in the midst of the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that every place where there were a thousand vines, at a thousand pieces of silver, shall become thorns and briers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With arrows and with bows they shall go in thither: for briers and thorns shall be in all the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as for all the hills that shall be raked with a rake, the fear of thorns and briers shall not come thither, but they shall be for the ox to feed on, and the lesser cattle to tread upon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to me: Take thee a great book, and write in it with a man’s pen. Take away the spoils with speed, quickly take the prey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I took unto me faithful witnesses, Urias the priest, and Zacharias the son of Barachias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I went to the prophetess, and she conceived, and bore a son. And the Lord said to me: Call his name, Hasten to take away the spoils: Make hast to take away the prey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For before the child know to call his father and his mother, the strength of Damascus, and the spoils of Samaria shall be taken away before the king of the Assyrians.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to me again, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Forasmuch as this people hath cast away the waters of Siloe, that go with silence, and hath rather taken Rasin, and the son of Romelia:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore behold the Lord will bring upon them the waters of the river strong and many, the king of the Assyrians, and all his glory: and he shall come up over all his channels, and shall overflow all his banks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall pass through Juda, overflowing, and going over shall reach even to the neck. And the stretching out of his wings shall fill the breadth of thy, land, O Emmanuel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gather yourselves together, O ye people, and be overcome, and give ear, all ye lands afar off: strengthen yourselves, and be overcome, gird yourselves, and be overcome.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take counsel together, and it shall be defeated: speak a word, and it shall not be done: because God is with us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord to me: As he hath taught me, with a strong arm, that I should not walk in the way of this people, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say ye not: A conspiracy: for all that this people speaketh, is a conspiracy: neither fear ye their fear, nor be afraid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sanctify the Lord of hosts himself: and let him be your fear, and let him be your dread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall be a sanctification to you. But for a stone of stumbling, and for a rock of offence to the two houses of Israel, for a snare and a ruin to the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And very many of them shall stumble and fall, and shall be broken in pieces, and shall be snared, and taken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bind up the testimony, seal the law among my disciples.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will wait for the Lord, who hath hid his face from the house of Jacob, and I will look for him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I and my children, whom the Lord hath given me for a sign, and for a wonder in Israel from the Lord of hosts, who dwelleth in mount Sion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they shall say to you: Seek of pythons, and of diviners, who mutter in their enchantments: should not the people seek of their God, for the living of the dead?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To the law rather, and to the testimony. And if they speak not according to this word, they shall not have the morning light.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall pass by it, they shall fall, and be hungry: and when they shall be hungry, they will be angry, and curse their king, and their God, and look upwards.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall look to the earth, and behold trouble and darkness, weakness and distress, and a mist following them, and they cannot fly away from their distress.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the first time the land of Zabulon, and the land of Nephtali was lightly touched: and at the last the way of the sea beyond the Jordan of the Galilee of the Gentiles was heavily loaded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The people that walked in darkness, have seen a great light: to them that dwelt in the region of the shadow of death, light is risen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast multiplied the nation, and hast not increased the joy. They shall rejoice before thee, as they that rejoice in the harvest, as conquerors rejoice after taking a prey, when they divide the spoils.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the yoke of their burden, and the rod of their shoulder, and the sceptre of their oppressor thou hast overcome, as in the day of Madian.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For every violent taking of spoils, with tumult, and garment mingled with blood, shall be burnt, and be fuel for the fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For a CHILD IS BORN to us, and a son is given to us, and the government is upon his shoulder: and his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, God the Mighty, the Father of the world to come, the Prince of Peace.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His empire shall be multiplied, and there shall be no end of peace: he shall sit upon the throne of David, and upon his kingdom; to establish it and strengthen it with judgment and with justice, from henceforth and for ever: the zeal of the Lord of hosts will perform this.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord sent a word into Jacob, and it hath lighted upon Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the people of Ephraim shall know, and the inhabitants of Samaria that say in the pride and haughtiness of their heart:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The bricks are fallen down, but we will build with square stones: they have cut down the sycamores, but we will change them for cedars.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord shall set up the enemies of Rasin over him, and shall bring on his enemies in a crowd:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Syrians from the east, and, the Philistines from the west: and they shall devour Israel with open mouth. For all this his indignation is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people are not returned to him who hath struck them, and have not sought after the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord shall destroy out of Israel the head and the tail, him that bendeth down, and him that holdeth back, in one day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The aged and honourable, he is the head: and the prophet that teacheth lies, he is the tail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that call this people blessed, shall cause them to err: and they that are called blessed, shall be thrown down, headlong.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore the Lord shall have no joy in their young men: neither shall he have mercy on their fatherless, and widows: for every one is a hypocrite and wicked, and every mouth hath spoken folly. For all this his indignation is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For wickedness is kindled as a fire, it shall devour the brier and the thorn: and shall kindle in the thicket of the forest, and it shall be wrapped up in smoke ascending on high.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By the wrath of the Lord of hosts the land is troubled, and the people shall be as fuel for the fire: no man shall spare his brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall turn to the right hand, and shall be hungry: and shall eat on the left hand, and shall not be filled: every one shall eat the flesh of his own arm: Manasses Ephraim, and Ephraim Manasses, and they together shall be against Juda.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After all these things his indignation is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to them that make wicked laws: and when they write, write injustice:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To oppress the poor in judgment, and do violence to the cause of the humble of my people: that widows might be their prey, and that they might rob the fatherless.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What will you do in the day of visitation, and of the calamity which cometh from afar? to whom will ye flee for help? and where will ye leave your glory?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That you be not bowed down under the bond, and fall with the slain? In all these things his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to the Assyrian, he is the rod and the staff of my anger, and my indignation is in their hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will send him to a deceitful nation, and I will give him a charge against the people of my wrath, to take away the spoils, and to lay hold on the prey, and to tread them down like the mire of the streets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he shall not take it so, and his heart shall not think so: but his heart shall be set to destroy, and to cut off nations not a few.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he shall say:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Are not my princes as so many kings? is not Calano as Charcamis: and Emath as Arphad? is not Samaria as Damascus?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As my hand hath found the kingdoms of the idol, so also their idols of Jerusalem, and of Samaria.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall I not, as I have done to Samaria and her idols, so do to Jerusalem and her idols?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass, that when the Lord shall have performed all his works in mount Sion, and in Jerusalem, I will visit the fruit of the proud heart of the king of Assyria, and the glory of the haughtiness of his eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he hath said: By the strength of my own hand I have done it, and by my own wisdom I have understood: and I have removed the bounds of the people, and have taken the spoils of the princes, and as a mighty man hath pulled down them that sat on high.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And my hand hath found the strength of the people as a nest; and as eggs are gathered, that are left, so have I gathered all the earth: and there was none that moved the wing, or opened the mouth, or made the least noise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall the axe boast itself against him that cutteth with it? or shall the saw exalt itself against him by whom it is drawn? as if a rod should lift itself up against him that lifteth it up, and a staff exalt itself, which is but wood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore the sovereign Lord, the Lord of hosts, shall send leanness among his fat ones: and under his glory shall be kindled a burning, as it were the burning of a fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the light of Israel shall be as a fire, and the Holy One thereof as a flame: and his thorns and his briers shall be set on fire, and shall be devoured in one day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the glory of his forest, and of his beautiful hill, shall be consumed from the soul even to the flesh, and he shall run away through fear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that remain of the trees of his forest shall be so few, that they shall easily be numbered, and a child shall write them down.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the remnant of Israel, and they that shall escape of the house of Jacob, shall lean no more upon him that striketh them: but they shall lean upon the Lord the Holy One of Israel, in truth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The remnant shall be converted, the remnant, I say, of Jacob, to the mighty God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For if thy people, O Israel, shall be as the sand of the sea, a remnant of them shall be converted, the consumption abridged shall overflow with justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord God of hosts shall make a consumption, and an abridgment in the midst of all the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore, thus saith the Lord the God of hosts: O my people that dwellest in Sion, be not afraid of the Assyrian: he shall strike thee with his rod, and he shall lift up his staff over thee in the way of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For yet a little and a very little while, and my indignation shall cease, and my wrath shall be upon their wickedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord of hosts shall raise up a scourge against him, according to the slaughter of Madian in the rock of Oreb, and his rod over the sea, and he shall lift it up in the way of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that his burden shall be taken away from off thy shoulder, and his yoke from off thy neck, and the yoke shall putrify at the presence of the oil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall come into Aiath, he shall pass into Magron: at Machmas he shall lay up his carriages.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have passed in haste, Gaba is our lodging: Rama was astonished, Gabaath of Saul fled away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lift up thy voice, O daughter of Gallim, attend, O Laisa, poor Anathoth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Medemena is removed: ye inhabitants of Gabim, take courage.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is yet day enough, to remain in Nobe: he shall shake his hand against the mountain of the daughter of Sion, the hill of Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the sovereign Lord of hosts shall break the earthen vessel with terror, and the tall of stature shall be cut down, and the lofty shall be humbled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the thickets of the forest shall be cut down with iron, and Libanus with its high ones shall fall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall come forth a rod out of the root of Jesse, and a flower shall rise up out of his root.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the spirit of the Lord shall rest upon him: the spirit of wisdom, and of understanding, the spirit of counsel, and of fortitude, the spirit of knowledge, and of godliness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall be filled with the spirit of the fear of the Lord, He shall not judge according to the sight of the eyes, nor reprove according to the hearing of the ears.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he shall judge the poor with justice, and shall reprove with equity for the meek of the earth: and he shall strike the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips he shall slay the wicked.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And justice shall be the girdle of his loins: and faith the girdle of his reins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The wolf shall dwell with the lamb: and the leopard shall lie down with the kid: the calf and the lion, and the sheep shall abide together, and a little child shall lead them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The calf and the bear shall feed: their young ones shall rest together: and the lion shall eat straw like the ox.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sucking child shall play on the hole of the asp: and the weaned child shall thrust his hand into the den of the basilisk.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall not hurt, nor shall they kill in all my holy mountain, for the earth is filled with the knowledge of the Lord, as the covering waters of the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day the root of Jesse, who standeth for an ensign of the people, him the Gentiles shall beseech, and his sepulchre shall be glorious.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the Lord shall set his hand the second time to possess the remnant of his people, which shall be left from the Assyrians, and from Egypt, and from Phetros, and from Ethiopia, and from Elam, and from Sennaar, and from Emath, and from the islands of the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall set up a standard unto the nations, and shall assemble the fugitives of Israel, and shall gather together the dispersed of Juda from the four quarters of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the envy of Ephraim shall be taken away, and the enemies of Juda shall perish: Ephraim shall not envy Juda, and Juda shall not fight against Ephraim.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they shall fly upon the shoulders of the Philistines by the sea, they together shall spoil the children of the east: Edom, and Moab shall be under the rule of their hand, and the children of Ammon shall be obedient.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord shall lay waste the tongue of the sea of Egypt, and shall lift up his hand over the river in the strength of his spirit: and he shall strike it in the seven streams, so that men may pass through it in their shoes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be a highway for the remnant of my people, which shall be left from the Assyrians: as there was for Israel in the day that he came up out of the land of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt say in that day: I will give thanks to thee, O Lord, for thou wast angry with me: thy wrath is turned away, and thou hast comforted me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, God is my saviour, I will deal confidently, and will not fear: because the Lord is my strength, and my praise, and he is become my salvation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shall draw waters with joy out of the saviour’s fountains:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall say in that day: Praise ye the Lord, and call upon his name: make his works known among the people: remember that his name is high.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sing ye to the Lord, for he hath done great things: shew this forth in all the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rejoice, and praise, O thou habitation of Sion: for great is he that is in the midst of thee, the Holy One of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden of Babylon which Isaias the son of Amos saw.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Upon the dark mountain lift ye up a banner, exalt the voice, lift up the hand, and let the rulers go into the gates.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have commanded my sanctified ones, and have called my strong ones in my wrath, them that rejoice in my glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The noise of a multitude in the mountains, as it were of many people, the noise of the sound of kings, of nations gathered together: the Lord of hosts hath given charge to the troops of war.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To them that come from a country afar off, from the end of heaven: the Lord and the instruments of his wrath, to destroy the whole land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Howl ye, for the day of the Lord is near: it shall come as a destruction from the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore shall all hands be faint, and every heart of man shall melt,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall be broken. Gripings and pains, shall take hold of them, they shall be in pain as a woman in labour. Every one shall be amazed at his neighbour, their countenances shall be as faces burnt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, the day of the Lord shall come, a cruel day, and full of indignation, and of wrath, and fury, to lay the land desolate, and to destroy the sinners thereof out of it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the stars of heaven, and their brightness shall not display their light: the sun shall be darkened in his rising, and the moon shall not shine with her light.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will visit the evils of the world, and against the wicked for their iniquity: and I will make the pride of infidels to cease, and will bring down the arrogancy of the mighty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A man shall be more precious than gold, yea a man than the finest of gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For this I will trouble the heaven: and the earth shall be moved out of her place, for the indignation of the Lord of hosts, and for the day of his fierce wrath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be as a doe fleeing away, and as a sheep: and there shall be none to gather them together: every man shall turn to his own people, and every one shall flee to his own land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every one that shall be found, shall be slain: and every one that shall come to their aid, shall fall by the sword.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their infants shall be dashed in pieces before their eyes: their houses shall be pillaged, and their wives shall be ravished.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I will stir up the Medes against them, who shall not seek silver, nor desire gold:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But with their arrows they shall kill the children, and shall have no pity upon the sucklings of the womb, and their eye shall not spare their sons.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that Babylon, glorious among kingdoms, the famous pride of the Chaldeans, shall be even as the Lord destroyed Sodom and Gomorrha.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It shall no more be inhabited for ever, and it shall not be founded unto generation and generation: neither shall the Arabian pitch his tents there, nor shall shepherds rest there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But wild beasts shall rest there, and their houses shall be filled with serpents, and ostriches shall dwell there, and the hairy ones shall dance there:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And owls shall answer one another there, in the houses thereof, and sirens in the temples of pleasure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Her time is near at hand, and her days shall not be prolonged. For the Lord will have mercy on Jacob, and will yet choose out of Israel, and will make them rest upon their own ground: and the stranger shall be joined with them, and shall adhere to the house of Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people shall take them, and bring them into their place: and the house of Israel shall possess them in the land of the Lord for servants and handmaids: and they shall make them captives that had taken them, and shall subdue their oppressors.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that when God shall give thee rest from thy labour, and from thy vexation, and from the hard bondage, wherewith thou didst serve before,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take up this parable against the king of Babylon, and shalt say: How is the oppressor come to nothing, the tribute hath ceased?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord hath broken the staff of the wicked, the rod of the rulers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That struck the people in wrath with an incurable wound, that brought nations under in fury, that persecuted in a cruel manner.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The whole earth is quiet and still, it is glad and hath rejoiced.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fir trees also have rejoiced over thee, and the cedars of Libanus, saying: Since thou hast slept, there hath none come up to cut us down.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hell below was in an uproar to meet thee at thy coming, it stirred up the giants for thee. All the princes of the earth are risen up from their thrones, all the princes of nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All shall answer, and say to thee: Thou also art wounded as well as we, thou art become like unto us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy pride is brought down to hell, thy carcass is fallen down: under thee shall the moth be strewed, and worms shall be thy covering.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How art thou fallen from heaven, O Lucifer, who didst rise in the morning? how art thou fallen to the earth, that didst wound the nations?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou saidst in thy heart: I will ascend into heaven, I will exalt my throne above the stars of God, I will sit in the mountain of the covenant, in the sides of the north.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will ascend above the height of the clouds, I will be like the most High.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But yet thou shalt be brought down to hell, into the depth of the pit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that shall see thee, shall turn toward thee, and behold thee. Is this the man that troubled the earth, that shook kingdoms,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That made the world a wilderness, and destroyed the cities thereof, that opened not the prison to his prisoners?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the kings of the nations have all of them slept in glory, every one in his own house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou art cast out of thy grave, as an unprofitable branch defiled, and wrapped up among them that were slain by the sword, and art gone down to the bottom of the pit, as a rotten carcass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not keep company with them, even in burial: for thou hast destroyed thy land, thou hast slain thy people: the seed of the wicked shall not be named for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Prepare his children for slaughter for the iniquity of their fathers: they shall not rise up, nor inherit the land, nor fill the face of the world with cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will rise up against them, saith the Lord of hosts: and I will destroy the name of Babylon, and the remains, and the bud, and the offspring, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make it a possession for the ericius and pools of waters, and I will sweep it and wear it out with a besom, saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord of hosts hath sworn, saying: Surely as I have thought, so shall it be: and as I have purposed,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So shall it fall out: That I will destroy the Assyrian in my land, and upon my mountains tread him under foot: and his yoke shall be taken away from them, and his burden shall be taken off their shoulder.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the counsel, that I have purposed upon all the earth, and this is the hand that is stretched out upon all nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord of hosts hath decreed, and who can disannul it? and his hand is stretched out: and who shall turn it away?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the year that king Achaz died, was this burden:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rejoice not thou, whole Philistia, that the rod of him that struck thee is broken in pieces: for out of the root of the serpent shall come forth a basilisk, and his seed shall swallow the bird.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the firstborn of the poor shall be fed, and the poor shall rest with confidence: and I will make thy root perish with famine, and I will kill thy remnant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Howl, O gate; cry, O city: all Philistia is thrown down: for a smoke shall come from the north, and there is none that shall escape his troop.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And what shall be answered to the messengers of the nations? That the Lord hath founded Sion, and the poor of his people shall hope in him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden of Moab. Because in the night Ar of Moab is laid waste, it is silent: because the wall of Moab is destroyed in the night, it is silent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The house is gone up, and Dibon to the high places to mourn over Nabo, and over Medaba, Moab hath howled: on all their heads shall be baldness, and every beard shall be shaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In their streets they are girded with sackcloth: on the tops of their houses, and in their streets all shall howl and come down weeping.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hesebon shall cry, and Eleale, their voice is heard even to Jasa. For this shall the well appointed men of Moab howl, his soul shall howl to itself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My heart shall cry to Moab, the bars thereof shall flee unto Segor a heifer of three years old: for by the ascent of Luith they shall go up weeping: and in the way of Oronaim they shall lift up a cry of destruction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the waters of Nemrim shall be desolate, for the grass is withered away, the spring is faded, all the greenness is perished.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: According to the greatness of their work, is their visitation also: they shall lead them to the torrent of the willows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the cry is gone round about the border of Moab: the howling thereof unto Gallim, and unto the well of Elim the cry thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the waters of Dibon are filled with blood: for I will bring more upon Dibon: the lion upon them that shall flee of Moab, and upon the remnant of the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Send forth, O Lord, the lamb, the ruler of the earth, from Petra of the desert, to the mount of the daughter of Sion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass, that as a bird fleeing away, and as young ones flying out of the nest, so shall the daughters of Moab be in the passage of Arnon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take counsel, gather a council: make thy shadow as the night in the midday: hide them that flee, and betray not them that wander about.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My fugitives shall dwell with thee: O Moab, be thou a covert to them from the face of the destroyer: for the dust is at an end, the wretch is consumed: he hath failed, that trod the earth under foot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a throne shall be prepared in mercy, and one shall sit upon it in truth in the tabernacle of David, judging and seeking judgment and quickly rendering that which is just.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We have heard of the pride of Moab, he is exceeding proud: his pride and his arrogancy, and his indignation is more than his strength.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore shall Moab howl to Moab, every one shall howl: to them that rejoice upon the brick walls, tell ye their stripes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the suburbs of Hesebon are desolate, and the lords of the nations have destroyed the vineyard of Sabama: the branches thereof have reached even to Jazer: they have wandered in the wilderness, the branches thereof are left, they are gone over the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I will lament with the weeping of Jazer the vineyard of Sabama: I will water thee with my tears, O Hesebon, and Eleale: for the voice of the treaders hath rushed in upon thy vintage, and upon thy harvest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And gladness and joy shall be taken away from Carmel, and there shall be no rejoicing nor shouting in the vineyards. He shall not tread out wine in the press that was wont to tread it out: the voice of the treaders I have taken away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore my bowels shall sound like a harp for Moab, and my inward parts for the brick wall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass, when it is seen that Moab is wearied on his high places, that he shall go in to his sanctuaries to pray, and shall not prevail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the word, that the Lord spoke to Moab from that time:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now the Lord hath spoken, saying: In three years, as the years of a hireling, the glory of Moab shall be taken away for all the multitude of the people, and it shall be left small and feeble, not many.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden of Damascus. Behold Damascus shall cease to be a city, and shall be as a ruinous heap of stones.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The cities of Aroer shall be left for flocks, and they shall rest there, and there shall be none to make them afraid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And aid shall cease from Ephraim, and the kingdom from Damascus: and the remnant of Syria shall be as the glory of the children of Israel: saith the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the glory of Jacob shall be made thin, and the fatness of his flesh shall grow lean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall be as when one gathereth in the harvest that which remaineth, and his arm shall gather the ears of corn: and it shall be as he that seeketh ears in the vale of Raphaim.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the fruit thereof that shall be left upon it, shall be as one cluster of grapes, and as the shaking of the olive tree, two or three berries in the top of a bough, or four or five upon the top of the tree, saith the Lord the God of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day man shall bow down himself to his Maker, and his eyes shall look to the Holy One of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
