text: And he shall not look to the altars which his hands made; and he shall not have respect to the things that his fingers wrought, such as groves and temples.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day his strong cities shall be forsaken, as the ploughs, and the corn that were left before the face of the children of Israel, and thou shalt be desolate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because thou hast forgotten God thy saviour, and hast not remembered thy strong helper: therefore shalt thou plant good plants, and shalt sow strange seed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the day of thy planting shall be the wild grape, and in the morning thy seed shall flourish: the harvest is taken away in the day of inheritance, and shall grieve thee much.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to the multitude of many people, like the multitude of the roaring sea: and the tumult of crowds, like the noise of many waters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nations shall make a noise like the noise of waters overflowing, but he shall rebuke him, and he shall flee far off: and he shall be carried away as the dust of the mountains before the wind, and as a whirlwind before a tempest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the time of the evening, behold there shall be trouble: the morning shall come, and he shall not be: this is the portion of them that have wasted us, and the lot of them that spoiled us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to the land, the winged cymbal, which is beyond the rivers of Ethiopia,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That sendeth ambassadors by the sea, and in vessels of bulrushes upon the waters. Go, ye swift angels, to a nation rent and torn in pieces: to a terrible people, after which there is no other: to a nation expecting and trodden underfoot, whose land the rivers have spoiled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All ye inhabitants of the world, who dwell on the earth, when the sign shall be lifted up on the mountains, you shall see, and you shall hear the sound of the trumpet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord to me: I will take my rest, and consider in my place, as the noon light is clear, and as a cloud of dew in the day of harvest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For before the harvest it was all flourishing, and it shall bud without perfect ripeness, and the sprigs thereof shall be cut off with pruning hooks: and what is left shall be cut away and shaken out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be left together to the birds of the mountains, and the beasts of the earth: and the fowls shall be upon them all the summer, and all the beasts of the earth shall winter upon them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that time shall a present be brought to the Lord of hosts, from a people rent and torn in pieces: from a terrible people, after which there hath been no other: from a nation expecting, expecting and trodden under foot, whose land the rivers have spoiled, to the place of the name of the Lord of hosts, to mount Sion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden of Egypt. Behold the Lord will ascend upon a swift cloud, and will enter into Egypt, and the idols of Egypt shall be moved at his presence, and the heart of Egypt shall melt in the midst thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will set the Egyptians to fight against the Egyptians: and they shall fight brother against brother, and friend against friend, city against city, kingdom against kingdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the spirit of Egypt shall be broken in the bowels thereof, and I will cast down their counsel: and they shall consult their idols, and their diviners, and their wizards, and soothsayers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will deliver Egypt into the hand of cruel masters, and a strong king shall rule over them, saith the Lord the God of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the water of the sea shall be dried up, and the river shall be wasted and dry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the rivers shall fail: the streams of the banks shall be diminished, and be dried up. The reed and the bulrush shall wither away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The channel of the river shall be laid bare from its fountain, and every thing sown by the water shall be dried up, it shall wither away, and shall be no more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fishers also shall mourn, and all that cast a hook into the river shall lament, and they that spread nets upon the waters shall languish away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall be confounded that wrought in flax, combing and weaving fine linen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And its watery places shall be dry, all they shall mourn that made pools to take fishes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The princes of Tanis are become fools, the wise counsellors of Pharao have given foolish counsel: how will you say to Pharao: I am the son of the wise, the son of ancient kings?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Where are now thy wise men? let them tell thee, and shew what the Lord of hosts hath purposed upon Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The princes of Tanis are become fools, the princes of Memphis are gone astray, they have deceived Egypt, the stay of the people thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord hath mingled in the midst thereof the spirit of giddiness: and they have caused Egypt to err in all its works, as a drunken man staggereth and vomiteth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be no work for Egypt, to make head or tail, him that bendeth down, or that holdeth back.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day Egypt shall be like unto women, and they shall be amazed, and afraid, because of the moving of the hand of the Lord of hosts, which he shall move over it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the land of Juda shall be a terror to Egypt: everyone that shall remember it shall tremble because of the counsel of the Lord of hosts, which he hath determined concerning it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day there shall be five cities in the land of Egypt, speaking the language of Chanaan, and swearing by the Lord of hosts: one shall be called the city of the sun.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day there shall be an altar of the Lord in the midst of the land of Egypt, and a monument of the Lord at the borders thereof:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It shall be for a sign, and for a testimony to the Lord of hosts in the land of Egypt. For they shall cry to the Lord because of the oppressor, and he shall send them a Saviour and a defender to deliver them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord shall be known by Egypt, and the Egyptians shall know the Lord in that day, and shall worship him with sacrifices and offerings: and they shall make vows to the Lord, and perform them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord shall strike Egypt with a scourge, and shall heal it, and they shall return to the Lord, and he shall be pacified towards them, and heal them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day there shall be a way from Egypt to the Assyrians, and the Assyrian shall enter into Egypt, and the Egyptian to the Assyrians, and the Egyptians shall serve the Assyrian.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day shall Israel be the third to the Egyptian and the Assyrian: a blessing in the midst of the land,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which the Lord of hosts hath blessed, saying: Blessed be my people of Egypt, and the work of my hands to the Assyrian: but Israel is my inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the year that Tharthan entered into Azotus, when Sargon the king of the Assyrians had sent him, and he had fought against Azotus, and had taken it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that same time the Lord spoke by the hand of Isaias the son of Amos, saying Go, and loose the sackcloth from off thy loins, and take off thy shoes from thy feet. And he did so, and went naked, and barefoot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said: As my servant Isaias hath walked, naked and barefoot, it shall be a sign and a wonder of three years upon Egypt, and upon Ethiopia,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So shall the king of the Assyrians lead away the prisoners of Egypt, and the captivity of Ethiopia, young and old, naked and barefoot, with their buttocks uncovered to the shame of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be afraid, and ashamed of Ethiopia their hope, and of Egypt their glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the inhabitants of this isle shall say in that day: Lo this was our hope, to whom we fled for help, to deliver us from the face of the king of the Assyrians: and how shall we be able to escape?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden of the desert of the sea. As whirlwinds come from the south, it cometh from the desert from a terrible land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A grievous vision is told me: he that is unfaithful dealeth unfaithfully: and he that is a spoiler, spoileth. Go up, O Elam, besiege, O Mede: I have made all the mourning thereof to cease.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore are my loins filled with pain, anguish hath taken hold of me, as the anguish of a woman in labour: I fell down at the hearing of it, I was troubled at the seeing of it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My heart failed, darkness amazed me: Babylon my beloved is become a wonder to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Prepare the table, behold in the watchtower them that eat and drink: arise, ye princes, take up the shield.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus hath the Lord said to me: Go, and set a watchman: and whatsoever he shall see, let him tell.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he saw a chariot with two horsemen, a rider upon an ass, and a rider upon a camel: and he beheld them diligently with much heed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a lion cried out: I am upon the watchtower of the Lord, standing continually by day: and I am upon my ward, standing whole nights.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold this man cometh, the rider upon the chariot with two horsemen, and he answered, and said: Babylon is fallen, she is fallen, and all the graven gods thereof are broken unto the ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O my thrashing, and the children of my floor, that which I have heard of the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, I have declared unto you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden of Duma calleth to me out of Seir: Watchman, what of the night? watchman, what of the night?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The watchman said: The morning cometh, also the night: if you seek, seek: return, come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden in Arabia. In the forest at evening you shall sleep, in the paths of Dedanim.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Meeting the thirsty bring him water, you that inhabit the land of the south, meet with bread him that fleeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they are fled from before the swords, from the sword that hung over them, from the bent bow, from the face of a grievous battle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord to me: Within a year, according to the years of a hireling, all the glory of Cedar shall be taken away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the residue of the number of strong archers of the children of Cedar shall be diminished: for the Lord the God of Israel hath spoken it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden of the valley of vision. What aileth thee also, that thou too art wholly gone up to the housetops?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Full of clamour, a populous city, a joyous city: thy slain are not slain by the sword, nor dead in battle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the princes are fled together, and are bound hard: all that were found, are bound together, they are fled far off.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore have I said: Depart from me, I will weep bitterly: labour not to comfort me, for the devastation of the daughter of my people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For it is a day of slaughter and of treading down, and of weeping to the Lord the God of hosts in the valley of vision, searching the wall, and magnificent upon the mountain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Elam took the quiver, the chariot of the horseman, and the shield was taken down from the wall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thy choice valleys shall be full of chariots, and the horsemen shall place themselves in the gate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the covering of Juda shall be discovered, and thou shalt see in that day the armoury of the house of the forest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall see the breaches of the city of David, that they are many: and you have gathered together the waters of the lower pool,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And have numbered the houses of Jerusalem, and broken down houses to fortify the wall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you made a ditch between the two walls for the water of the old pool: and you have not looked up to the maker thereof, nor regarded him even at a distance, that wrought it long ago.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord, the God of hosts, in that day shall call to weeping, and to mourning, to baldness, and to girding with sackcloth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold joy and gladness, killing calves, and slaying rams, eating flesh, and drinking wine: Let us eat and drink; for to morrow we shall die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the voice of the Lord of hosts was revealed in my ears: Surely this iniquity shall not be forgiven you till you die, saith the Lord God of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord God of hosts: Go, get thee in to him that dwelleth in the tabernacle, to Sobna who is over the temple: and thou shalt say to him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What dost thou here, or as if thou wert somebody here? for thou hast hewed thee out a sepulchre here, thou hast hewed out a monument carefully in a high place, a dwelling for thyself in a rock.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the Lord will cause thee to be carried away, as a cock is carried away, and he will lift thee up as a garment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He will crown thee with a crown of tribulation, he will toss thee like a ball into a large and spacious country: there shalt thou die, and there shall the chariot of thy glory be, the shame of the house of thy Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will drive thee out from thy station, and depose thee from thy ministry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will call my servant Eliacim the son of Helcias,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will clothe him with thy robe, and will strengthen him with thy girdle, and will give thy power into his hand: and he shall be as a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to the house of Juda.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will lay the key of the house of David upon his shoulder: and he shall open, and none shall shut: and he shall shut, and none shall open.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will fasten him as a peg in a sure place, and he shall be for a throne of glory to the house of his father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father’s house, divers kinds of vessels, every little vessel, from the vessels of cups even to every instrument of music.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day, saith the Lord of hosts, shall the peg be removed, that was fastened in the sure place: and it shall be broken and shall fall: and that which hung thereon, shall perish, because the Lord hath spoken it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden of Tyre. Howl, ye ships of the sea, for the house is destroyed, from whence they were wont to come: from the land of Cethim it is revealed to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be silent, you that dwell in the island: the merchants of Sidon passing over the sea, have filled thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The seed of the Nile in many waters, the harvest of the river is her revenue: and she is become the mart of the nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be thou ashamed, O Sidon: for the sea speaketh, even the strength of the sea, saying: I have not been in labour, nor have I brought forth, nor have I nourished up young men, nor brought up virgins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When it shall be heard in Egypt, they will be sorry when they shall hear of Tyre:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Pass over the seas, howl, ye inhabitants of the island.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is not this your city, which gloried from of old in her antiquity? her feet shall carry her afar off to sojourn.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath taken this counsel against Tyre, that was formerly crowned, whose merchants were princes, and her traders the nobles of the earth?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord of hosts hath designed it, to pull down the pride of all glory, and bring to disgrace all the glorious ones of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Pass thy land as a river, O daughter of the sea, thou hast a girdle no more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He stretched out his hand over the sea, he troubled kingdoms: the Lord hath given a charge against Chanaan, to destroy the strong ones thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Thou shalt glory no more, O virgin daughter of Sidon, who art oppressed: arise and sail over to Cethim, there also thou shalt have no rest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the land of the Chaldeans, there was not such a people, the Assyrians founded it: they have led away the strong ones thereof into captivity, they have destroyed the houses thereof, they have, brought it to ruin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Howl, O ye ships of the sea, for your strength is laid waste.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day that thou, O Tyre, shalt be forgotten, seventy years, according to the days of one king: but after seventy years, there shall be unto Tyre as the song of a harlot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take a harp, go about the city, harlot that hast been forgotten: sing well, sing many a song, that thou mayst be remembered.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass after seventy years, that the Lord will visit Tyre, and will bring her back again to her traffic: and she shall commit fornication again with all the kingdoms of the world upon the face of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And her merchandise and her hire shall be sanctified to the Lord: they shall not be kept in store, nor laid up: for her merchandise shall be for them that shall dwell before the Lord, that they may eat unto fulness, and be clothed for a continuance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the Lord shall lay waste the earth, and shall strip it, and shall afflict the face thereof, and scatter abroad the inhabitants thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall be as with the people, so with the priest: and as with the servant so with his master: as with the handmaid, so with her mistress: as with the buyer, so with the seller: as with the lender, so with the borrower: as with him that calleth for his money, so with him that oweth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With desolation shall the earth be laid waste, and it shall be utterly spoiled: for the Lord hath spoken this word.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The earth mourned, and faded away, and is weakened: the world faded away, the height of the people of the earth is weakened.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the earth is infected by the inhabitants thereof: because they have transgressed the laws, they have changed the ordinance, they have broken the everlasting covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore shall a curse devour the earth, and the inhabitants thereof shall sin: and therefore they that dwell therein shall be mad, and few men shall be left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The vintage hath mourned, the vine hath languished away, all the merry-hearted have sighed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The mirth of timbrels hath ceased, the noise of them that rejoice is ended, the melody of the harp is silent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall not drink wine with a song: the drink shall be bitter to them that drink it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The city of vanity is broken down, every house is shut up, no man cometh in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There shall be a crying for wine in the streets: all mirth is forsaken: the joy of the earth is gone away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Desolation is left in the city, and calamity shall oppress the gates.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For it shall be thus in the midst of the earth, in the midst of the people, as if a few olives, that remain, should be shaken out of the olive tree: or grapes, when the vintage is ended.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These shall lift up their voice, and shall give praise: when the Lord shall be glorified, they shall make a joyful noise from the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore glorify ye the Lord in instruction: the name of the Lord God of Israel in the islands of the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From the ends of the earth we have heard praises, the glory of the just one. And I said: My secret to myself, my secret to myself, woe is me: the prevaricators have prevaricated, and with the prevarication of transgressors they have prevaricated.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear, and the pit, and the snare are upon thee, O thou inhabitant of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass, that he that shall flee from the noise of the fear, shall fall into the pit: and he that shall rid himself out of the pit, shall be taken in the snare: for the flood-gates from on high are opened, and the foundations of the earth shall be shaken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With breaking shall the earth be broken, with crushing shall the earth be crushed, with trembling shall the earth be moved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With shaking shall the earth be shaken as a drunken man, and shall be removed as the tent of one night: and the iniquity thereof shall be heavy upon it, and it shall fall, and not rise again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass, that in that day the Lord shall visit upon the host of heaven on high, and upon the kings of the earth, on the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be gathered together as in the gathering of one bundle into the pit, and they shall be shut up there in prison: and after many days they shall be visited.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the moon shall blush, and the sun shall be ashamed, when the Lord of hosts shall reign in mount Sion, and in Jerusalem, and shall be glorified in the sight of his ancients.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord, thou art my God, I will exalt thee, and give glory to thy name: for thou hast done wonderful things, thy designs of old faithful, amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thou hast reduced the city to a heap, the strong city to ruin, the house of strangers, to be no city, and to be no more built up for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore shall a strong people praise thee, the city of mighty nations shall fear thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because thou hast been a strength to the poor, a strength to the needy in his distress: a refuge from the whirlwind, a shadow from the heat. For the blast of the mighty is like a whirlwind beating against a wall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt bring down the tumult of strangers, as heat in thirst: and as with heat under a burning cloud, thou shalt make the branch of the mighty to wither away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord of hosts shall make unto all people in this mountain, a feast of fat things, a feast of wine, of fat things full of marrow, of wine purified from the lees.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall destroy in this mountain the face of the bond with which all people were tied, and the web that he began over all nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall cast death down headlong for ever: and the Lord God shall wipe away tears from every face, and the reproach of his people he shall take away from off the whole earth: for the Lord hath spoken it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall say in that day: Lo, this is our God, we have waited for him, and he will save us: this is the Lord, we have patiently waited for him, we shall rejoice and be joyful in his salvation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the hand of the Lord shall rest in this mountain: and Moab shall be trodden down under him, as straw is broken in pieces with the wain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall stretch forth his hands under him, as he that swimmeth stretcheth forth his hands to swim: and he shall bring down his glory with the dashing of his hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the bulwarks of thy high walls shall fall, and be brought low, and shall be pulled down to the ground, even to the dust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day shall this canticle be sung in the land of Juda. Sion the city of our strength a saviour, a wall and a bulwark shall be set therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Open ye the gates, and let the just nation, that keepeth the truth, enter in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The old error is passed away: thou wilt keep peace: peace, because we have hoped in thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have hoped in the Lord for evermore, in the Lord God mighty for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he shall bring down them that dwell on high, the high city he shall lay low. He shall bring it down even to the ground, he shall pull it down even to the dust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The foot shall tread it down, the feet of the poor, the steps of the needy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The way of the just is right, the path of the just is right to walk in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the way of thy judgments, O Lord, we have patiently waited for thee: thy name, and thy remembrance are the desire of the soul.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My soul hath desired thee in the night: yea, and with my spirit within me in the morning early I will watch to thee. When thou shalt do thy judgments on the earth, the inhabitants of the world shall learn justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let us have pity on the wicked, but he will not learn justice: in the land of the saints he hath done wicked things, and he shall not see the glory of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lord, let thy hand be exalted, and let them not see: let the envious people see, and be confounded: and let fire devour thy enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lord, thou wilt give us peace: for thou hast wrought all our works for us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord our God, other lords besides thee have had dominion over us, only in thee let us remember thy name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let not the dead live, let not the giants rise again: therefore hast visited and destroyed them, and hast destroyed all their memory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast been favourable to the nation, O Lord, thou hast been favourable to the nation: art thou glorified? thou hast removed all the ends of the earth far off.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lord, they have sought after thee in distress, in the tribulation of murmuring thy instruction was with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As a woman with child, when she draweth near the time of her delivery, is in pain, and crieth out in her pangs: so are we become in thy presence, O Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We have conceived, and been as it were in labour, and have brought forth wind: we have not wrought salvation on the earth, therefore the inhabitants of the earth have not fallen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy dead men shall live, my slain shall rise again: awake, and give praise, ye that dwell in the dust: for thy dew is the dew of the light: and the land of the giants thou shalt pull down into ruin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go, my people, enter into thy chambers, shut thy doors upon thee, hide thyself a little for a moment, until the indignation pass away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For behold the Lord will come out of his place, to visit the iniquity of the inhabitant of the earth against him: and the earth shall disclose her blood, and shall cover her slain no more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day the Lord with his hard, and great, and strong sword shall visit leviathan the bar serpent, and leviathan the crooked serpent, and shall slay the whale that is in the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day there shall be singing to the vineyard of pure wine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am the Lord that keep it, I will suddenly give it drink: lest any hurt come to it, I keep it night and day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is no indignation in me: who shall make me a thorn and a brier in battle: shall I march against it, shall, I set it on fire together?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or rather shall it take hold of my strength, shall it make peace with me, shall it make peace with me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When they shall rush in unto Jacob, Israel shall blossom and bud, and they shall fill the face of the world with seed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hath he struck him according to the stroke of him that struck him? or is he slain, as he killed them that were slain by him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In measure against measure, when it shall be cast off, thou shalt judge it. He hath meditated with his severe spirit in the day of heat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore upon this shall the iniquity of the house of Jacob be forgiven: and this is all the fruit, that the sin thereof should be taken away, when he shall have made all the stones of the altar, as burnt stones broken in pieces, the groves and temples shall not stand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the strong city shall be desolate, the beautiful city shall be forsaken, and shall be left as a wilderness: there the calf shall feed, and there shall he lie down, and shall consume its branches.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Its harvest shall be destroyed with drought, women shall come and teach it: for it is not a wise people, therefore he that made it, shall not have mercy on it: and he that formed it, shall not spare it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass, that in that day the Lord will strike from the channel of the river even to the torrent of Egypt, and you shall be gathered together one by one, O ye children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass, that in that day a noise shall be made with a great trumpet, and they that were lost, shall come from the land of the Assyrians, and they that were outcasts in the land of Egypt, and they shall adore the Lord in the holy mount in Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to the crown of pride, to the drunkards of Ephraim, and to the fading flower the glory his joy, who were on the head of the fat valley, staggering with wine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the Lord is mighty and strong, as a storm of hail: a destroying whirlwind, as the violence of many waters overflowing, and sent forth upon a spacious land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The crown of pride of the drunkards of Ephraim shall be trodden under feet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the fading flower the glory of his joy, who is on the head of the fat valley, shall be as a hasty fruit before the ripeness of autumn: which when he that seeth it shall behold, as soon as he taketh it in his hand, he will eat it up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day the Lord of hosts shall be a crown of glory, and a garland of joy to the residue of his people:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a spirit of judgment to him that sitteth in judgment, and strength to them that return out of the battle to the gate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But these also have been ignorant through wine, and through drunkenness have erred: the priest and the prophet have been ignorant through drunkenness, they are swallowed up with wine, they have gone astray in drunkenness, they have not known him that seeth, they have been ignorant of judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For all the tables were full of vomit and filth, so that there was no more place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whom shall he teach knowledge? and whom shall he make to understand the hearing? them that are weaned from the milk, that are drawn away from the breasts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For command, command again; command, command again; expect, expect again; expect, expect again; a little there, a little there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For with the speech of lips, and with another tongue he will speak to this people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To whom he said: This is my rest, refresh the weary, and this is my refreshing: and they would not hear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the word of the Lord shall be to them: Command, command again; command, command again; expect, expect again; a little there, a little there: that they may go, and fall backward, and be broken, and snared, and taken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore hear the word of the Lord, ye scornful men, who rule over my people that is in Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For you have said: We have entered into a league with death, and we have made a covenant with hell. When the overflowing scourge shall pass through, it shall not come upon us: for we have placed our hope in lies, and by falsehood we are protected.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore thus saith the Lord God: Behold I will lay a stone in the foundations of Sion, a tried stone, a corner stone, a precious stone, founded in the foundation. He that believeth, let him not hasten.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will set judgment in weight, and justice in measure: and hail shall overturn the hope of falsehood: and waters shall overflow its protection.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And your league with death shall be abolished, and your covenant with hell shall not stand: when the overflowing scourge shall pass, you shall be trodden down by it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whensoever it shall pass through, it shall take you away: because in the morning early it shall pass through, in the day and in the night, and vexation alone shall make you understand what you hear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the bed is straitened, so that one must fall out, and a short covering cannot cover both.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord shall stand up as in the mountain of divisions: he shall be angry as in the valley which is in Gabaon: that he may do his work, his strange work: that he may perform his work, his work is strange to him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now do not mock, lest your bonds be tied strait. For I have heard of the Lord the God of hosts a consumption and a cutting short upon all the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give ear, and hear my voice, hearken, and hear my speech.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall the ploughman plough all the day to sow, shall he open and harrow his ground?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Will he not, when he hath made plain the surface thereof, sow gith, and scatter cummin, and put wheat in order, and barley, and millet, and vetches in their bounds?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he will instruct him in judgment: his God will teach him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For gith shall not be thrashed with saws, neither shall the cart wheel turn about upon cummin: but gith shall be beaten out with a rod, and cumin with a staff.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But breadcorn shall be broken small: but the thrasher shall not thrash it for ever, neither shall the cart wheel hurt it, nor break it with its teeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This also is come forth from the Lord God of hosts, to make his counsel wonderful, and magnify justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to Ariel, to Ariel the city which David took: year is added to year: the solemnities are at an end.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make a trench about Ariel, and it shall be in sorrow and mourning, and it shall be to me as Ariel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make a circle round about thee, and I will cast up a rampart against thee, and raise up bulwarks to besiege thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt be brought down, thou shalt speak out of the earth, and thy speech shall be heard out of the ground: and thy voice shall be from the earth like that of the python, and out of the earth thy speech shall mutter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the multitude of them that fan thee, shall be like small dust: and as ashes passing away, the multitude of them that have prevailed against thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall be at an instant suddenly. A visitation shall come from the Lord of hosts in thunder, and with earthquake, and with a great noise of whirlwind and tempest; and with the flame of devouring fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the multitude of all nations that have fought against Ariel, shall be as the dream of a vision by night, and all that have fought, and besieged and prevailed against it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as he that is hungry dreameth, and eateth, but when he is awake, his soul is empty: and as he that is thirsty dreameth, and drinketh and after he is awake, is yet faint with thirst, and his soul is empty: so shall be the multitude of all the Gentiles, that have fought against mount Sion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be astonished, and wonder, waver, and stagger: be drunk, and not with wine: stagger, and not with drunkenness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord hath mingled for you the spirit of a deep sleep, he will shut up your eyes, he will cover your prophets and princes, that see visions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the vision of all shall be unto you as the words of a book that is sealed which when they shall deliver to one that is learned, they shall say: Read this: and he shall answer: I cannot, for it is sealed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the book shall be given to one that knoweth no letters, and it shall be said to him: Read: and he shall answer: I know no letters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said: Forasmuch as this people draw near me with their mouth, and with their lips glorify me, but their heart is far from me, and they have feared me with the commandment and doctrines of men:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore behold I will proceed to cause an admiration in this people, by a great and wonderful miracle: for wisdom shall perish from their wise men, and the understanding of their prudent men shall be hid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you that are deep of heart, to hide your counsel from the Lord: and their works are in the dark, and they say: Who seeth us, and who knoweth us?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This thought of yours is perverse: as if the clay should think against the potter, and the work should say to the maker thereof: Thou madest me not: or the thing framed should say to him that fashioned it: Thou understandest not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is it not yet a very little while, and Libanus shall be turned into charmel, and charmel shall be esteemed as a forest?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in that day the deaf shall hear the words of the book, and out of darkness and obscurity the eyes of the blind shall see.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the meek shall increase their joy in the Lord, and the poor men shall rejoice in the Holy One of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he that did prevail hath failed, the scorner is consumed, and they are all cut off that watched for iniquity:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That made men sin by word, and supplanted him that reproved them in the gate, and declined in vain from the just.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore thus saith the Lord to the house of Jacob, he that redeemed Abraham: Jacob shall not now be confounded, neither shall his countenance now be ashamed:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when he shall see his children, the work of my hands in the midst of him sanctifying my name, and they shall sanctify the Holy One of Jacob, and shall glorify the God of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that erred in spirit, shall know understanding, and they that murmured, shall learn the law.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you, apostate children, saith the Lord, that you would take counsel, and not of me: and would begin a web, and not by my spirit, that you might add sin upon sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who walk to go down into Egypt, and have not asked at my mouth, hoping for help in the strength of Pharao, and trusting in the shadow of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the strength of Pharao shall be to your confusion, and the confidence of the shadow of Egypt to your shame.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thy princes were in Tanis, and thy messengers came even to Hanes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They were all confounded at a people that could not profit them: they were no help, nor to any profit, but to confusion and to reproach.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The burden of the beasts of the south. In a land of trouble and distress, from whence come the lioness, and the lion, the viper and the flying basilisk, they carry their riches upon the shoulders of beasts, and their treasures upon the bunches of camels to a people that shall not be able to profit them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For Egypt shall help in vain, and to no purpose: therefore have I cried concerning this: It is pride only, sit still.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now therefore go in and write for them upon box, and note it diligently in a book, and it shall be in the latter days for a testimony for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For it is a people that provoketh to wrath, and lying children, children that will not hear the law of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who say to the seers: See not: and to them that behold: Behold not for us those things that are right: speak unto us pleasant things, see errors for us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take away from me the way, turn away the path from me, let the Holy One of Israel cease from before us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore thus saith the Holy One of Israel: Because you have rejected this word, and have trusted in oppression and tumult, and have leaned upon it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore shall this iniquity be to you as a breach that falleth, and is found wanting in a high wall, for the destruction thereof shall come on a sudden, when it is not looked for.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall be broken small, as the potter’s vessel is broken all to pieces with mighty breaking, and there shall not a sherd be found of the pieces thereof, wherein a little fire may be carried from the hearth, or a little water be drawn out of the pit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord God the Holy One of Israel: If you return and be quiet, you shall be saved: in silence and in hope shall your strength be. And you would not:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But have said: No, but we will flee to horses: therefore shall you flee. And we will mount upon swift ones: therefore shall they be swifter that shall pursue after you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A thousand men shall flee for fear of one: and for fear of five shall you flee, till you be left as the mast of ship on the top of a mountain, and as an ensign upon a hill.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore the Lord waiteth that he may have mercy on you: and therefore shall he be exalted sparing you: because the Lord is the God of judgment: blessed are all they that wait for him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the people of Sion shall dwell in Jerusalem: weeping thou shalt not weep, he will surely have pity on thee: at the voice of thy cry, as soon as he shall hear, he will answer thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord will give you spare bread, and short water: and will not cause thy teacher to flee away from thee any more, and thy eyes shall see thy teacher.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thy ears shall hear the word of one admonishing thee behind thy back: This is the way, walk ye in it: and go not aside neither to the right hand, nor to the left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt defile the plates of thy graven things of silver, and the garment of thy molten things of gold, and shalt cast them away as the uncleanness of a menstruous woman. Thou shalt say to it: Get thee hence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And rain shall be given to thy seed, wheresoever thou shalt sow in the land: and the bread of the corn of the land shall be most plentiful, and fat. The lamb in that day shall feed at large in thy possession:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thy oxen, and the ass colts that till the ground, shall eat mingled provender as it was winnowed in the floor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be upon every high mountain, and upon every elevated hill rivers of running waters in the day of the slaughter of many, when the tower shall fall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the light of the moon shall be as the light of the sun, and the light of the sun shall be sevenfold, as the light of seven days: in the day when the Lord shall bind up the wound of his people, and shall heal the stroke of their wound.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the name of the Lord cometh from afar, his wrath burneth, and is heavy to bear: his lips are filled with indignation, and his tongue as a devouring fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His breath as a torrent overflowing even to the midst of the neck, to destroy the nations unto nothing, and the bridle of error that was in the jaws of the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall have a song as in the night of the sanctified solemnity, and joy of heart, as when one goeth with a pipe, to come into the mountain of the Lord, to the Mighty One of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord shall make the glory of his voice to be heard, and shall shew the terror of his arm, in the threatening of wrath, and the flame of devouring fire: he shall crush to pieces with whirlwind, and hailstones.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For at the voice of the Lord the Assyrian shall fear being struck with the rod.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the passage of the rod shall be strongly grounded, which the Lord shall make to rest upon him with timbrels and harps, and in great battles he shall overthrow them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For Topheth is prepared from yesterday, prepared by the king, deep, and wide. The nourishment thereof is fire and much wood: the breath of the Lord as a torrent of brimstone kindling it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to them that go down to Egypt for help, trusting in horses, and putting their confidence in chariots, because they are many: and in horsemen, because they are very strong: and have not trusted in the Holy One of Israel, and have not sought after the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he that is the wise one hath brought evil, and hath not removed his words: and he will rise up against the house of the wicked, and against the aid of them that work iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Egypt is man, and not God: and their horses, flesh, and not spirit: and the Lord shall put down his hand, and the helper shall fall, and he that is helped shall fall, and they shall all be confounded together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord to me: Like as the lion roareth, and the lion’s whelp upon his prey, and when a multitude of shepherds shall come against him, he will not fear at their voice, nor be afraid of their multitude: so shall the Lord of hosts come down to fight upon mount Sion, and upon the hill thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As birds flying, so will the Lord of hosts protect Jerusalem, protecting and delivering, passing over and saving.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Return as you had deeply revolted, O children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For in that day a man shall cast away his idols of silver, and his idols of gold, which your hands have made for you to sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Assyrian shall fall by the sword not of a man, and the sword not of a man shall devour him, and he shall flee not at the face of the sword, and his young men shall be tributaries.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his strength shall pass away with dread, and his princes fleeing shall be afraid: the Lord hath said it, whose fire is in Sion, and his furnace in Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold a king shall reign in justice, and princes shall rule in judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a man shall be as when one is hid from the wind, and hideth himself from a storm, as rivers of waters in drought, and the shadow of a rock that standeth out in a desert land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The eyes of them that see shall not be dim, and the ears of them that hear shall hearken diligently.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the heart of fools shall understand knowledge, and the tongue of stammerers shall speak readily and plain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fool shall no more be called prince: neither shall the deceitful be called great:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the fool will speak foolish things, and his heart will work iniquity, to practise hypocrisy, and speak to the Lord deceitfully, and to make empty the soul of the hungry, and take away drink from the thirsty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The vessels of the deceitful are most wicked: for he hath framed devices to destroy the meek, with lying words, when the poor man speaketh judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the prince will devise such things as are worthy of a prince, and he shall stand above the rulers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rise up, ye rich women, and hear my voice: ye confident daughters, give ear to my speech.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For after days and a year, you that are confident shall be troubled: for the vintage is at an end, the gathering shall come no more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be astonished, ye rich women, be troubled, ye confident ones: strip you, and be confounded, gird your loins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Mourn for your breasts, for the delightful country, for the fruitful vineyard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Upon the land of my people shall thorns and briers come up: how much more upon all the houses of joy, of the city that rejoiced?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the house is forsaken, the multitude of the city is left, darkness and obscurity are come upon its dens for ever. A joy of wild asses, the pastures of flocks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Until the spirit be poured upon us from on high: and the desert shall be as a charmel, and charmel shall be counted for a forest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And judgment shall dwell in the wilderness, and justice shall sit in charmel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the work of justice shall be peace, and the service of justice quietness, and security for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And my people shall sit in the beauty of peace, and in the tabernacles of confidence, and in wealthy rest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But hail shall be in the descent of the forest, and the city shall be made very low.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are ye that sow upon all waters, sending thither the foot of the ox and the ass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to thee that spoilest, shalt not thou thyself also be spoiled? and thou that despisest, shalt not thyself also be despised? when thou shalt have made an end of spoiling, thou shalt be spoiled: when being wearied thou shalt cease to despise, thou shalt be despised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord, have mercy on us: for we have waited for thee: be thou our arm in the morning, and our salvation in the time of trouble.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the voice of the angel the people fled, and at the lifting up thyself the nations are scattered.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And your spoils shall be gathered together as the locusts are gathered, as when the ditches are full of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord is magnified, for he hath dwelt on high: he hath filled Sion with judgment and justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be faith in thy times: riches of salvation, wisdom and knowledge: the fear of the Lord is his treasure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold they that see shall cry without, the angels of peace shall weep bitterly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The ways are made desolate, no one passeth by the road, the covenant is made void, he hath rejected the cities, he hath not regarded the men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The land hath mourned, and languished: Libanus is confounded, and become foul, and Saron is become as a desert: and Basan and Carmel are shaken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now will I rise up, saith the Lord: now will I be exalted, now will I lift up myself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall conceive heat, you shall bring forth stubble: your breath as fire shall devour you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people shall be as ashes after a fire, as a bundle of thorns they shall be burnt with fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, you that are far off, what I have done, and you that are near know my strength.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sinners in Sion are afraid, trembling hath seized upon the hypocrites. Which of you can dwell with devouring fire? which of you shall dwell with everlasting burnings?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that walketh in justices, and speaketh truth, that casteth away avarice by oppression, and shaketh his hands from all bribes, that stoppeth his ears lest he hear blood, and shutteth his eyes that he may see no evil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall dwell on high, the fortifications of rocks shall be his highness: bread is given him, his waters are sure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His eyes shall see the king in his beauty, they shall see the land far off.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy heart shall meditate fear: where is the learned? where is he that pondereth the words of the law? where is the teacher of little ones?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The shameless people thou shalt not see, the people of profound speech: so that thou canst not understand the eloquence of his tongue, in whom there is no wisdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Look upon Sion the city of our solemnity: thy eyes shall see Jerusalem, a rich habitation, a tabernacle that cannot be removed: neither shall the nails thereof be taken away for ever, neither shall any of the cords thereof be broken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because only there our Lord is magnificent: a place of rivers, very broad and spacious streams: no ship with oars shall pass by it, neither shall the great galley pass through it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord is our judge, the Lord is our lawgiver, the Lord is our king: he will save us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy tacklings are loosed, and they shall be of no strength: thy mast shall be in such condition, that thou shalt not be able to spread the flag. Then shall the spoils of much prey be divided: the lame shall take the spoil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shall he that is near, say: I am feeble. The people that dwell therein, shall have their iniquity taken away from them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Come near, ye Gentiles, and hear, and hearken, ye people: let the earth hear, and all that is therein, the world, and every thing that cometh forth of it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the indignation of the Lord is upon all nations, and his fury upon all their armies: he hath killed them, and delivered them to slaughter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their slain shall be cast forth, and out of their carcasses shall rise a stink: the mountains shall be melted with their blood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the host of the heavens shall pine away, and the heavens shall be folded together as a book: and all their host shall fall down as the leaf falleth from the vine, and from the fig tree.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For my sword is inebriated in heaven: behold it shall come down upon Idumea, and upon the people of my slaughter unto judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sword of the Lord is filled with blood, it is made thick with the blood of lambs and buck goats, with the blood of rams full of marrow: for there is a victim of the Lord in Bosra and a great slaughter in the land of Edom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the unicorns shall go down with them, and the bulls with the mighty: their land shall be soaked with blood, and their ground with the fat of fat ones.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For it is the day of the vengeance of the Lord, the year of recompenses of the judgment of Sion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the streams thereof shall be turned into pitch, and the ground thereof into brimstone: and the land thereof shall become burning pitch.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Night and day it shall not be quenched, the smoke thereof shall go up for ever: from generation to generation it shall lie waste, none shall pass through it for ever and ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The bittern and ericius shall possess it: and the ibis and the raven shall dwell in it: and a line shall be stretched out upon it, to bring it to nothing, and a plummet, unto desolation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The nobles thereof shall not be there: they shall call rather upon the king, and all the princes thereof shall be nothing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thorns and nettles shall grow up in its houses, and the thistle in the fortresses thereof: and it shall be the habitation of dragons, and the pasture of ostriches.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And demons and monsters shall meet, and the hairy ones shall cry out one to another, there hath the lamia lain down, and found rest for herself.
