source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There hath the ericius had its hole, and brought up its young ones, and hath dug round about, and cherished them in the shadow thereof: thither are the kites gathered together one to another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Search ye diligently in the book of the Lord, and read: not one of them was wanting, one hath not sought for the other: for that which proceedeth out of my mouth, he hath commanded, and his spirit it hath gathered them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he hath cast the lot for them, and his hand hath divided it to them by line: they shall possess it for ever, from generation to generation they shall dwell therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The land that was desolate and impassable shall be glad, and the wilderness shall rejoice, and shall flourish like the lily.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It shall bud forth and blossom, and shall rejoice with joy and praise: the glory of Libanus is given to it: the beauty of Carmel, and Saron, they shall see the glory of the Lord, and the beauty of our God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Strengthen ye the feeble hands, and confirm the weak knees.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say to the fainthearted: Take courage, and fear not: behold your God will bring the revenge of recompense: God himself will come and will save you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall the eyes of the blind be opened, and the ears of the deaf shall be unstopped.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall the lame man leap as a hart, and the tongue of the dumb shall be free: for waters are broken out in the desert, and streams in the wilderness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that which was dry land, shall become a pool, and the thirsty land springs of water. In the dens where dragons dwelt before, shall rise up the verdure of the reed and the bulrush.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a path and a way shall be there, and it shall be called the holy way: the unclean shall not pass over it, and this shall be unto you a straight way, so that fools shall not err therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No lion shall be there, nor shall any mischievous beast go up by it, nor be found there: but they shall walk there that shall be delivered.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the redeemed of the Lord shall return, and shall come into Sion with praise, and everlasting joy shall be upon their heads: they shall obtain joy and gladness, and sorrow and mourning shall flee away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass in the fourteenth year of king Ezechias, that Sennacherib king of the Assyrians came up against all the fenced cities of Juda, and took them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king of the Assyrians sent Rabsaces from Lachis to Jerusalem, to king Ezechias with a great army, and he stood by the conduit of the upper pool in the way of the fuller’s field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there went out to him Eliacim the son of Helcias, who was over the house, and Sobna the scribe, and Joahe the son of Asaph the recorder.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Rabsaces said to them: Tell Ezechias: Thus saith the great king, the king of the Assyrians: What is this confidence wherein thou trustest?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or with what counsel or strength dost thou prepare for war? on whom dost thou trust, that thou art revolted from me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lo thou trustest upon this broken staff of a reed, upon Egypt: upon which if a man lean, it will go into his hand, and pierce it: so is Pharao king of Egypt to all that trust in him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thou wilt answer me: We trust in the Lord our God: is it not he whose high places and altars Ezechias hath taken away, and hath said to Juda and Jerusalem: You shall worship before this altar?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now deliver thyself up to my lord the king of the Assyrians, and I will give thee two thousand horses, and thou wilt not be able on thy part to find riders for them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And how wilt thou stand against the face of the judge of one place, of the least of my master’s servants? But if thou trust in Egypt, in chariots and in horsemen:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And am I now come up without the Lord against this land to destroy it? The Lord said to me: Go up against this land, and destroy it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Eliacim, and Sobna, and Joahe said to Rabsaces: Speak to thy servants in the Syrian tongue: for we understand it: speak not to us in the Jews’ language in the hearing of the people, that are upon the wall.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Rabsaces said to them: Hath my master sent me to thy master and to thee, to speak all these words; and not rather to the men that sit on the wall; that they may eat their own dung, and drink their urine with you?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Rabsaces stood, and cried out with a loud voice in the Jews’ language, and said: Hear the words of the great king, the king of the Assyrians.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the king: Let not Ezechias deceive you, for he shall not be able to deliver you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And let not Ezechias make you trust in the Lord, saying: The Lord will surely deliver us, and this city shall not be given into the hands of the king of the Assyrians.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do not hearken to Ezechias: for thus said the king of the Assyrians: Do with me that which is for your advantage, and come out to me, and eat ye every one of his vine, and every one of his fig tree, and drink ye every one the water of his cistern,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Till I come and take you away to a land, like to your own, a land of corn and of wine, a land of bread and vineyards.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither let Ezechias trouble you, saying: The Lord will deliver us. Have any of the gods of the nations delivered their land out of the hand of the king of the Assyrians?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Where is the god of Emath and of Arphad? where is the god of Sepharvaim? have they delivered Samaria out of my hand?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is there among all the gods of these lands, that hath delivered his country out of my hand, that the Lord may deliver Jerusalem out of my hand?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they held their peace, and answered him not a word. For the king had commanded, saying: Answer him not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Eliacim the son of Helcias, that was over the house, and Sobna the scribe, and Joahe the son of Asaph the recorder, went in to Ezechias with their garments rent, and told him the words of Rabsaces.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass, when king Ezechias had heard it, that he rent his garments and covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he sent Eliacim who was over the house, and Sobna the scribe, and the ancients of the priests covered with sackcloth, to Isaias the son of Amos the prophet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they said to him: Thus saith Ezechias: This day is a day of tribulation, and of rebuke, and of blasphemy: for the children are come to the birth, and there is not strength to bring forth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It may be the Lord thy God will hear the words of Rabsaces, whom the king of the Assyrians his master hath sent to blaspheme the living God, and to reproach with words which the Lord thy God hath heard: wherefore lift up by prayer for the remnant that is left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the servants of Ezechias came to Isaias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Isaias said to them: Thus shall you say to your master: Thus saith the Lord: Be not afraid of the words that thou hast heard, with which the servants of the king of the Assyrians have blasphemed me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, I will send a spirit upon him, and he shall hear a message, and shall return to his own country, and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own country.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Rabsaces returned, and found the king of the Assyrians besieging Lobna. For he had heard that he was departed from Lachis.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he heard say about Tharaca the king of Ethiopia: He is come forth to fight against thee. And when he heard it, he sent messengers to Ezechias, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus shall you speak to Ezechias the king of Juda, saying: Let not thy God deceive thee, in whom thou trustest, saying: Jerusalem shall not be given into the hands of the king of the Assyrians.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold thou hast heard all that the kings of the Assyrians have done to all countries which they have destroyed, and canst thou be delivered?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Have the gods of the nations delivered them whom my fathers have destroyed, Gozam, and Haram, and Reseph, and the children of Eden, that were in Thalassar?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Where is the king of Emath, and the king of Arphad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, of Ana, and of Ava?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ezechias took the letter from the hand of the messengers, and read it, and went up to the house of the Lord, and Ezechias spread it before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ezechias prayed to the Lord, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lord of hosts, God of Israel who sittest upon the cherubims, thou alone art the God of all the kingdoms of the earth, thou hast made heaven and earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Incline, O Lord, thy ear, and hear: open, O Lord, thy eyes, and see, and hear all the words of Sennacherib, which he hath sent to blaspheme the living God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For of a truth, O Lord, the kings of the Assyrians have laid waste lands, and their countries.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they have cast their gods into the fire, for they were not gods, but the works of men’s hands, of wood and stone: and they broke them in pieces.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now, O Lord our God, save us out of his hand: and let all the kingdoms of the earth know, that thou only art the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Isaias the son of Amos sent to Ezechias, saying: Thus saith the Lord the God of Israel: For the prayer thou hast made to me concerning Sennacherib the king of the Assyrians:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the word which the Lord hath spoken of him: The virgin the daughter of Sion hath despised thee, and laughed thee to scorn: the daughter of Jerusalem hath wagged the head after thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whom hast thou reproached, and whom hast thou blasphemed, and against whom hast thou exalted thy voice, and lifted up thy eyes on high? Against the Holy One of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By the hand of thy servants thou hast reproached the Lord: and hast said: With the multitude of my chariots I have gone up to the height of the mountains, to the top of Libanus: and I will cut down its tall cedars, and its choice fir trees, and will enter to the top of its height, to the forest of its Carmel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have digged, and drunk water, and have dried up with the sole of my foot, all the rivers shut up in banks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hast thou not heard what I have done to him of old? from the days of old I have formed it: and now I have brought it to effect: and it hath come to pass that hills fighting together, and fenced cities should be destroyed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The inhabitants of them were weak of hand, they trembled, and were confounded: they became like the grass of the field, and the herb of the pasture, and like the grass of the housetops, which withered before it was ripe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I know thy dwelling, and thy going out, and thy coming in, and thy rage against me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou wast mad against me, thy pride came up to my ears: therefore I will put a ring in thy nose, and a bit between thy lips, and I will turn thee back by the way by which thou camest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But to thee this shall be a sign: Eat this year the things that spring of themselves, and in the second year eat fruits: but in the third year sow and reap, and plant vineyards, and eat the fruit of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that which shall be saved of the house of Juda, and which is left, shall take root downward, and shall bear fruit upward:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant, and salvation from mount Sion: the zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore thus saith the Lord concerning the king of the Assyrians: He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow into it, nor come before it with shield, nor cast a trench about it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By the way that he came, he shall return, and into this city he shall not come, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will protect this city, and will save it for my own sake, and for the sake of David my servant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the angel of the Lord went out and slew in the camp of the Assyrians a hundred and eighty-five thousand. And they arose in the morning, and behold they were all dead corpses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Sennacherib the king of the Assyrians went out and departed, and returned, and dwelt in Ninive.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass, as he was worshipping in the temple of Nesroch his god, that Adramelech and Sarasar his sons slew him with the sword: and they fled into the land of Ararat, and Asarhaddon his son reigned in his stead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In those days Ezechias was sick even to death, and Isaias the son of Amos the prophet came unto him, and said to him: Thus saith the Lord: Take order with thy house, for thou shalt die, and not live.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ezechias turned his face toward the wall, and prayed to the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said: I beseech thee, O Lord, remember how I have walked before thee in truth, and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in thy sight. And Ezechias wept with great weeping.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the word of the Lord came to Isaias, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go and say to Ezechias: Thus saith the Lord the God of David thy father: I have heard thy prayer, and I have seen thy tears: behold I will add to thy days fifteen years:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will deliver thee and this city out of the hand of the king of the Assyrians, and I will protect it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this shall be a sign to thee from the Lord, that the Lord will do this word which he hath spoken:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I will bring again the shadow of the lines, by which it is now gone down in the sun dial of Achaz with the sun, ten lines backward. And the sun returned ten lines by the degrees by which it was gone down.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The writing of Ezechias king of Juda, when he had been sick, and was recovered of his sickness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I said: In the midst of my days I shall go to the gates of hell: I sought for the residue of my years.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I said: I shall not see the Lord God in the land of the living. I shall behold man no more, nor the inhabitant of rest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My generation is at an end, and it is rolled away from me, as a shepherd’s tent. My life is cut off, as by a weaver: whilst I was yet but beginning, he cut me off: from morning even to night thou wilt make an end of me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I hoped till morning, as a lion so hath he broken all my bones: from morning even to night thou wilt make an end of me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will cry like a young swallow, I will meditate like a dove: my eyes are weakened looking upward: Lord, I suffer violence, answer thou for me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What shall I say, or what shall he answer for me, whereas he himself hath done it? I will recount to thee all my years in the bitterness of my soul.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord, if man’s life be such, and the life of my spirit be in such things as these, thou shalt correct me, and make me to live.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold in peace is my bitterness most bitter: but thou hast delivered my soul that it should not perish, thou hast cast all my sins behind thy back.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For hell shall not confess to thee, neither shall death praise thee: nor shall they that go down into the pit, look for thy truth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The living, the living, he shall give praise to thee, as I do this day: the father shall make thy truth known to the children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord, save me, and we will sing our psalms all the days of our life in the house of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now Isaias had ordered that they should take a lump of figs, and lay it as a plaster upon the wound, and that he should be healed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ezechias had said: What shall be the sign that I shall go up to the house of the Lord?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that time Merodach Baladan, the son of Baladan king of Babylon, sent letters and presents to Ezechias: for he had heard that he had been sick and was recovered.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ezechias rejoiced at their coming, and he shewed them the storehouses of his aromatical spices, and of the silver, and of the gold, and of the sweet odours, and of the precious ointment, and all the storehouses of his furniture, and all things that were found in his treasures. There was nothing in his house, nor in all his dominion that Ezechias shewed them not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Isaias the prophet came to king Ezechias, and said to him: What said these men, and from whence came they to thee? And Ezechias said: From a far country they came to me, from Babylon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: What saw they in thy house? And Ezechias said: All things that are in my house have they seen, there was not any thing which I have not shewn them in my treasures.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Isaias said to Ezechias: Hear the word of the Lord of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the days shall come that all that is in thy house, and that thy fathers have laid up in store until this day, shall be carried away into Babylon: there shall not any thing be left, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of thy children, that shall issue from thee, whom thou shalt beget, they shall take away, and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ezechias said to Isaias: The word of the Lord, which he hath spoken, is good. And he said: Only let peace and truth be in my days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be comforted, be comforted, my people, saith your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak ye to the heart of Jerusalem, and call to her: for her evil is come to an end, her iniquity is forgiven: she hath received of the hand of the Lord double for all her sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The voice of one crying in the desert: Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight in the wilderness the paths of our God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low, and the crooked shall become straight, and the rough ways plain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh together shall see, that the mouth of the Lord hath spoken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The voice of one, saying: Cry. And I said: What shall I cry? All flesh is grass, and all the glory thereof as the flower of the field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The grass is withered, and the flower is fallen, because the spirit of the Lord hath blown upon it. Indeed the people is grass:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The grass is withered, and the flower is fallen: but the word of our Lord endureth for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Get thee up upon a high mountain, thou that bringest good tidings to Sion: lift up thy voice with strength, thou that bringest good tidings to Jerusalem: lift it up, fear not. Say to the cities of Juda: Behold your God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the Lord God shall come with strength, and his arm shall rule: Behold his reward is with him and his work is before him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather together the lambs with his arm, and shall take them up in his bosom, and he himself shall carry them that are with young.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and weighed the heavens with his palm? who hath poised with three fingers the bulk of the earth, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath forwarded the spirit of the Lord? or who hath been his counsellor, and hath taught him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With whom hath he consulted, and who hath instructed him, and taught him the path of justice, and taught him knowledge, and shewed him the way of understanding?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the Gentiles are as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the smallest grain of a balance: behold the islands are as a little dust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Libanus shall not be enough to burn, nor the beasts thereof sufficient for a burnt offering.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All nations are before him as if they had no being at all, and are counted to him as nothing, and vanity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To whom then have you likened God? or what image will you make for him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hath the workman cast a graven statue? or hath the goldsmith formed it with gold, or the silversmith with plates of silver?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath chosen strong wood, and that will not rot: the skilful workman seeketh how he may set up an idol that may not be moved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do you not know? hath it not been heard? hath it not been told you from the beginning? have you not understood the foundations of the earth?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is he that sitteth upon the globe of the earth, and the inhabitants thereof are as locusts: he that stretcheth out the heavens as nothing, and spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that bringeth the searchers of secrets to nothing, that hath made the judges of the earth as vanity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And surely their stock was neither planted, nor sown, nor rooted in the earth: suddenly he hath blown upon them, and they are withered, and a whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to whom have ye likened me, or made me equal, saith the Holy One?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lift up your eyes on high, and see who hath created these things: who bringeth out their host by number, and calleth them all by their names: by the greatness of his might, and strength, and power, not one of them was missing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why sayest thou, O Jacob, and speakest, O Israel: My way is hid from the Lord, and my judgment is passed over from my God?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Knowest thou not, or hast thou not heard? the Lord is the everlasting God, who hath created the ends of the earth: he shall not faint, nor labour, neither is there any searching out of his wisdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is he that giveth strength to the weary, and increaseth force and might to them that are not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Youths shall faint, and labour, and young men shall fall by infirmity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they that hope in the Lord shall renew their strength, they shall take wings as eagles, they shall run and not be weary, they shall walk and not faint.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the islands keep silence before me, and the nations take new strength: let them come near, and then speak, let us come near to judgment together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath raised up the just one from the east, hath called him to follow him? he shall give the nations in his sight, and he shall rule over kings: he shall give them as the dust to his sword, as stubble driven by the wind, to his bow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall pursue them, he shall pass in peace, no path shall appear after his feet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath wrought and done these things, calling the generations from the beginning? I the Lord, I am the first and the last.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The islands saw it, and feared, the ends of the earth were astonished, they drew near, and came.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every one shall help his neighbour, and shall say to his brother: Be of good courage.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The coppersmith striking with the hammer encouraged him that forged at that time, saying: It is ready for soldering: and he strengthened it with nails, that it should not be moved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou Israel, art my servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, the seed of Abraham my friend:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In whom I have taken thee from the ends of the earth, and from the remote parts thereof have called thee, and said to thee: Thou art my servant, I have chosen thee, and have not cast thee away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear not, for I am with thee: turn not aside, for I am thy God: I have strengthened thee, and have helped thee, and the right hand of my just one hath upheld thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold all that fight against thee shall be confounded and ashamed, they shall be as nothing, and the men shall perish that strive against thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt seek them, and shalt not find the men that resist thee: they shall be as nothing: and as a thing consumed the men that war against thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I am the Lord thy God, who take thee by the hand, and say to thee: Fear not, I have helped thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear not, thou worm of Jacob, you that are dead of Israel: I have helped thee, saith the Lord: and thy Redeemer the Holy One of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have made thee as a new thrashing wain, with teeth like a saw: thou shalt thrash the mountains, and break them in pieces: and shalt make the hills as chaff.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt fan them, and the wind shall carry them away, and the whirlwind shall scatter them: and thou shalt rejoice in the Lord, in the Holy One of Israel thou shalt be joyful.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The needy and the poor seek for waters, and there are none: their tongue hath been dry with thirst. I the Lord will hear them, I the God of Israel will not forsake them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will open rivers in the high hills, and fountains in the midst of the plains: I will turn the desert into pools of waters, and the impassable land into streams of waters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will plant in the wilderness the cedar, and the thorn, and the myrtle, and the olive tree: I will set in the desert the fir tree, the elm, and the box tree together:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That they may see and know, and consider, and understand together that the hand of the Lord hath done this, and the Holy One of Israel hath created it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bring your cause near, saith the Lord: bring hither, if you have any thing to allege, saith the King of Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let them come, and tell us all things that are to come: tell us the former things what they were: and we will set our heart upon them and shall know the latter end of them, and tell us the things that are to come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shew the things that are to come hereafter, and we shall know that ye are gods. Do ye also good or evil, if you can: and let us speak, and see together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, you are of nothing, and your work of that which hath no being: he that hath chosen you is an abomination.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have raised up one from the north, and he shall come from the rising of the sun: he shall call upon my name, and he shall make princes to be as dirt, and as the potter treading clay.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath declared from the beginning, that we may know: and from time of old, that we may say: Thou art just. There is none that sheweth, nor that foretelleth, nor that heareth your words.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The first shall say to Sion: Behold they are here, and to Jerusalem I will give an evangelist.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I saw, and there was no one even among them to consult, or who, when I asked, could answer a word.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold they are all in the wrong, and their works are vain: their idols are wind and vanity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold my servant, I will uphold him: my elect, my soul delighteth in him: I have given my spirit upon him, he shall bring forth judgment to the Gentiles.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall not cry, nor have respect to person, neither shall his voice be heard abroad.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The bruised reed he shall not break, and smoking flax he shall not quench, he shall bring forth judgment unto truth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall not be sad, nor troublesome, till he set judgment in the earth, and the islands shall wait for his law.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord God that created the heavens, and stretched them out: that established the earth, and the things that spring out of it: that giveth breath to the people upon it, and spirit to them that tread thereon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I the Lord have called thee in justice, and taken thee by the hand, and preserved thee. And I have given thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou mightest open the eyes of the blind, and bring forth the prisoner out of prison, and them that sit in darkness out of the prison house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I the Lord, this is my name: I will not give my glory to another, nor my praise to graven things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The things that were first, behold they are come: and new things do I declare: before they spring forth, I will make you hear them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sing ye to the Lord a new song, his praise is from the ends of the earth: you that go down to the sea, and all that are therein: ye islands, and ye inhabitants of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the desert and the cities thereof be exalted: Cedar shall dwell in houses: ye inhabitants of Petra, give praise, they shall cry from the top of the mountains.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall give glory to the Lord, and shall declare his praise in the islands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord shall go forth as a mighty man, as a man of war shall he stir up zeal: he shall shout and cry: he shall prevail against his enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have always held my peace, I have kept silence, I have been patient, I will speak now as a woman in labour: I will destroy, and swallow up at once.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will lay waste the mountains and hills, and will make all their grass to wither: and I will turn rivers into islands, and will dry up the standing pools.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will lead the blind into the way which they know not: and in the paths which they were ignorant of I will make them walk: I will make darkness light before them, and crooked things straight: these things have I done to them, and have not forsaken them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They are turned back: let them be greatly confounded, that trust in a graven thing, that say to a molten thing: You are our god.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, ye deaf, and, ye blind, behold that you may see.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is blind, but my servant? or deaf, but he to whom I have sent my messengers? Who is blind, but he that is sold? or who is blind, but the servant of the Lord?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou that seest many things, wilt thou not observe them? thou that hast ears open, wilt thou not hear?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord was willing to sanctify him, and to magnify the law, and exalt it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But this is a people that is robbed and wasted: they are all the snare of young men, and they are hid in the houses of prisons: they are made a prey, and there is none to deliver them: a spoil, and there is none that saith: Restore.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is there among you that will give ear to this, that will attend and hearken for times to come?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath given Jacob for a spoil, and Israel to robbers? hath not the Lord himself, against whom we have sinned? And they would not walk in his ways, and they have not hearkened to his law.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he hath poured out upon him the indignation of his fury, and a strong battle, and hath burnt him round about, and he knew not: and set him on fire, and he understood not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now thus saith the Lord that created thee, O Jacob, and formed thee, O Israel: Fear not, for I have redeemed thee, and called thee by thy name: thou art mine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou shalt pass through the waters, I will be with thee, and the rivers shall not cover thee: when thou shalt walk in the fire, thou shalt not be burnt, and the flames shall not burn in thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I am the Lord thy God, the Holy One of Israel, thy Saviour: I have given Egypt for thy atonement, Ethiopia and Saba for thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Since thou becamest honourable in my eyes, thou art glorious: I have loved thee, and I will give men for thee, and people for thy life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear not, for I am with thee: I will bring thy seed from the east, and gather thee from the west.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will say to the north: Give up: and to the south: Keep not back: bring my sons from afar, and my daughters from the ends of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And every one that calleth upon my name, I have created him for my glory. I have formed him, and made him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bring forth the people that are blind, and have eyes: that are deaf, and have ears.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the nations are assembled together, and the tribes are gathered: who among you can declare this, and shall make us hear the former things? let them bring forth their witnesses, let them be justified, and hear, and say: It is truth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You are my witnesses, saith the Lord, and my servant whom I have chosen: that you may know, and believe me, and understand that I myself am. Before me there was no God formed, and after me there shall be none.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am, I am the Lord: and there is no saviour besides me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have declared, and have saved. I have made it heard, and there was no strange one among you. You are my witnesses, saith the Lord, and I am God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from the beginning I am the same, and there is none that can deliver out of my hand: I will work, and who shall turn it away?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord your redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: For your sake I sent to Babylon, and have brought down all their bars, and the Chaldeans glorying in their ships.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am the Lord your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your King.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord, who made a way in the sea, and a path in the mighty waters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who brought forth the chariot and the horse, the army and the strong: they lay down to sleep together, and they shall not rise again: they are broken as flax, and are extinct.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember not former things, and look not on things of old.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I do new things, and now they shall spring forth, verily you shall know them: I will make a way in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The beast of the field shall glorify me, the dragons and the ostriches: because I have given waters in the wilderness, rivers in the desert, to give drink to my people, to my chosen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This people have I formed for myself, they shall shew forth my praise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou hast not called upon me, O Jacob, neither hast thou laboured about me, O Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast not offered me the ram of thy holocaust, nor hast thou glorified me with thy victims: I have not caused thee to serve with oblations, nor wearied thee with incense.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast bought me no sweet cane with money, neither hast thou filled me with the fat of thy victims. But thou hast made me to serve with thy sins, thou hast wearied me with thy iniquities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am, I am he that blot out thy iniquities for my own sake, and I will not remember thy sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Put me in remembrance, and let us plead together: tell if thou hast any thing to justify thyself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy first father sinned, and thy teachers have transgressed against me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I have profaned the holy princes, I have given Jacob to slaughter, and Israel to reproach.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now hear, O Jacob, my servant, and Israel whom I have chosen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord that made and formed thee, thy helper from the womb: Fear not, O my servant Jacob, and thou most righteous whom I have chosen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I will pour out waters upon the thirsty ground, and streams upon the dry land: I will pour out my spirit upon thy seed, and my blessing upon thy stock.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall spring up among the herbs, as willows beside the running waters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One shall say: I am the Lord’s, and another shall call himself by the name of Jacob, and another shall subscribe with his hand, To the Lord, and surname himself by the name of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord the king of Israel, and his redeemer the Lord of hosts: I am the first, and I am the last, and besides me there is no God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is like to me? let him call and declare: and let him set before me the order, since I appointed the ancient people: and the things to come, and that shall be hereafter, let them shew unto them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear ye not, neither be ye troubled from that time I have made thee to hear, and have declared: you are my witnesses. Is there a God besides me, a maker, whom I have not known?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The makers of idols are all of them nothing, and their best beloved things shall not profit them. They are their witnesses, that they do not see, nor understand, that they may be ashamed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath formed a god, and made a graven thing that is profitable for nothing?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, all the partakers thereof shall be confounded: for the makers are men: they shall all assemble together, they shall stand and fear, and shall be confounded together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The smith hath wrought with his file, with coals, and with hammers he hath formed it, and hath wrought with the strength of his arm: he shall hunger and faint, he shall drink no water, and shall be weary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The carpenter hath stretched out his rule, he hath formed it with a plane: he hath made it with corners, and hath fashioned it round with the compass: and he hath made the image of a man as it were a beautiful man dwelling in a house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath cut down cedars, taken the holm, and the oak that stood among the trees of the forest: he hath planted the pine tree, which the rain hath nourished.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it hath served men for fuel: he took thereof, and warmed himself: and he kindled it, and baked bread: but of the rest he made a god, and adored it: he made a graven thing, and bowed down before it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Part of it he burnt with fire, and with part of it he dressed his meat: he boiled pottage, and was filled, and was warmed, and said: Aha, I am warm, I have seen the fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the residue thereof he made a god, and a graven thing for himself: he boweth down before it, and adoreth it, and prayeth unto it, saying: Deliver me, for thou art my God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have not known, nor understood: for their eyes are covered that they may not see, and that they may not understand with their heart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They do not consider in their mind, nor know, nor have the thought to say: I have burnt part of it in the fire, and I have baked bread upon the coals thereof: I have broiled flesh and have eaten, and of the residue thereof shall I make an idol? shall I fall down before the stock of a tree?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Part thereof is ashes: his foolish heart adoreth it, and he will not save his soul, nor say: Perhaps there is a lie in my right hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember these things, O Jacob, and Israel, for thou art my servant. I have formed thee, thou art my servant, O Israel, forget me not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have blotted out thy iniquities as a cloud, and thy sins as a mist: return to me, for I have redeemed thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give praise, O ye heavens, for the Lord hath shewn mercy: shout with joy, ye ends of the earth: ye mountains, resound with praise, thou, O forest, and every tree therein: for the Lord hath redeemed Jacob, and Israel shall be glorified.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord thy redeemer, and thy maker, from the womb: I am the Lord, that make all things, that alone stretch out the heavens, that establish the earth, and there is none with me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That make void the tokens of diviners, and make the soothsayers mad. That turn the wise backward, and that make their knowledge foolish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That raise up the word of my servant and perform the counsel of my messengers, who say to Jerusalem: Thou shalt be inhabited: and to the cities of Juda: You shall be built, and I will raise up the wastes thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who say to the deep: Be thou desolate, and I will dry up thy rivers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who say to Cyrus: Thou art my shepherd, and thou shalt perform all my pleasure. Who say to Jerusalem: Thou shalt be built: and to the temple: Thy foundations shall be laid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord to my anointed Cyrus, whose right hand I have taken hold of, to subdue nations before his face, and to turn the backs of kings, and to open the doors before him, and the gates shall not be shut.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will go before thee, and will humble the great ones of the earth: I will break in pieces the gates of brass, and will burst the bars of iron.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will give thee hidden treasures, and the concealed riches of secret places: that thou mayest know that I am the Lord who call thee by thy name, the God of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the sake of my servant Jacob, and Israel my elect, I have even called thee by thy name: I have made a likeness of thee, and thou hast not known me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am the Lord, and there is none else: there is no God besides me: I girded thee, and thou hast not known me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That they may know who are from the rising of the sun, and they who are from the west, that there is none besides me. I am the Lord, and there is none else:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I form the light, and create darkness, I make peace, and create evil: I the Lord that do all these things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Drop down dew, ye heavens, from above, and let the clouds rain the just: let the earth be opened, and bud forth a saviour: and let justice spring up together: I the Lord have created him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to him that gainsayeth his maker, a sherd of the earthen pots: shall the clay say to him that fashioneth it: What art thou making, and thy work is without hands?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to him that saith to his father: Why begettest thou? and to the woman: Why dost thou bring forth?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord the Holy One of Israel, his maker: Ask me of things to come, concerning my children, and concerning the work of my hands give ye charge to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I made the earth: and I created man upon it: my hand stretched forth the heavens, and I have commanded all their host.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have raised him up to justice, and I will direct all his ways: he shall build my city, and let go my captives, not for ransom, nor for presents, saith the Lord the God of hosts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord: The labour of Egypt, and the merchandise of Ethiopia, and of Sabaim, men of stature shall come over to thee, and shall be thine: they shall walk after thee, they shall go bound with manacles: and they shall worship thee, and shall make supplication to thee: only in thee is God, and there is no God besides thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Verily thou art a hidden God, the God of Israel the saviour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They are all confounded and ashamed: the forgers of errors are gone together into confusion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Israel is saved in the Lord with an eternal salvation: you shall not be confounded, and you shall not be ashamed for ever and ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord that created the heavens, God himself that formed the earth, and made it, the very maker thereof: he did not create it in vain: he formed it to be inhabited. I am the Lord, and there is no other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have not spoken in secret, in a dark place of the earth: I have not said to the seed of Jacob: Seek me in vain. I am the Lord that speak justice, that declare right things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Assemble yourselves, and come, and draw near together, ye that are saved of the Gentiles: they have no knowledge that set up the wood of their graven work, and pray to a god that cannot save.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Tell ye, and come, and consult together: who hath declared this from the beginning, who hath foretold this from that time? Have not I the Lord, and there is no God else besides me? A just God and a saviour, there is none besides me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be converted to me, and you shall be saved, all ye ends of the earth: for I am God, and there is no other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have sworn by myself, the word of justice shall go out of my mouth, and shall not return:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For every knee shall be bowed to me, and every tongue shall swear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore shall he say: In the Lord are my justices and empire: they shall come to him, and all that resist him shall be confounded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the Lord shall all the seed of Israel be justified and praised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bel is broken, Nebo is destroyed: their idols are put upon beasts and cattle, your burdens of heavy weight even unto weariness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They are consumed, and are broken together: they could not save him that carried them, and they themselves shall go into captivity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hearken unto me, O house of Jacob, all the remnant of the house of Israel who are carried by my bowels, are borne up by my womb.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even to your old age I am the same, and to your grey hairs I will carry you: I have made you, and I will bear: I will carry and will save.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To whom have you likened me, and made me equal, and compared me, and made me like?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You that contribute gold out of the bag, and weigh out silver in the scales: and hire a goldsmith to make a god: and they fall down and worship.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They bear him on their shoulders and carry him, and set him in his place, and he shall stand, and shall not stir out of his place. Yea, when they shall cry also unto him, he shall not hear: he shall not save them from tribulation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember this, and be ashamed: return, ye transgressors, to the heart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember the former age, for I am God, and there is no God beside, neither is there the like to me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who shew from the beginning the things that shall be at last, and from ancient times the things that as yet are not done, saying: My counsel shall stand, and all my will shall be done:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who call a bird from the east, and from a far country the man of my own will, and I have spoken, and will bring it to pass: I have created, and I will do it. Hear me, O ye hardhearted, who are far from justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have brought my justice near, it shall not be afar off: and my salvation shall not tarry. I will give salvation in Sion, and my glory in Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Come down, sit in the dust, O virgin daughter of Babylon, sit on the ground: there is no throne for the daughter of the Chaldeans, for thou shalt no more be called delicate and tender.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take a millstone and grind meal: uncover thy shame, strip thy shoulder, make bare thy legs, pass over the rivers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy nakedness shall be discovered, and thy shame shall be seen: I will take vengeance, and no man shall resist me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Our redeemer, the Lord of hosts is his name, the Holy One of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sit thou silent, and get thee into darkness, O daughter of the Chaldeans: for thou shalt no more be called the lady of kingdoms.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I was angry with my people, I have polluted my inheritance, and have given them into thy hand: thou hast shewn no mercy to them: upon the ancient thou hast laid thy yoke exceeding heavy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou hast said: I shall be a lady for ever: thou hast not laid these things to thy heart, neither hast thou remembered thy latter end.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now hear these things, thou that art delicate, and dwellest confidently, that sayest in thy heart: I am, and there is none else besides me: I shall not sit as a widow, and I shall not know barrenness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These two things shall come upon thee suddenly in one day, barrenness and widowhood. All things are come upon thee, because of the multitude of thy sorceries, and for the great hardness of thy enchanters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou hast trusted in thy wickedness, and hast said: There is none that seeth me. Thy wisdom, and, thy knowledge, this hath deceived thee. And thou hast said in thy heart: I am, and besides me there is no other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Evil shall come upon thee, and thou shalt not know the rising thereof: and calamity shall fall violently upon thee, which thou canst not keep off: misery shall come upon thee suddenly, which thou shalt not know.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Stand now with thy enchanters, and with the multitude of thy sorceries, in which thou hast laboured from thy youth, if so be it may profit thee any thing, or if thou mayst become stronger.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast failed in the multitude of thy counsels: let now the astrologers stand and save thee, they that gazed at the stars, and counted the months, that from them they might tell the things that shall come to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold they are as stubble, fire hath burnt them, they shall not deliver themselves from the power of the flames: there are no coals wherewith they may be warmed, nor fire, that they may sit thereat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Such are all the things become to thee, in which thou hast laboured: thy merchants from thy youth, every one hath erred in his own way, there is none that can save thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear ye these things, O house of Jacob, you that are called by the name of Israel, and are come forth out of the waters of Juda, you who swear by the name of the Lord, and make mention of the God of Israel, but not in truth, nor in justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they are called of the holy city, and are established upon the God of Israel: the Lord of hosts is his name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The former things of old, I have declared, and they went forth out of my mouth, and I have made them to be heard: I did them suddenly and they came to pass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I knew that thou art stubborn, and thy neck is as an iron sinew, and thy forehead as brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I foretold thee of old, before they came to pass I told thee, lest thou shouldst say: My idols have done these things, and my graven and molten things have commanded them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: See now all the things which thou hast heard: but have you declared them? I have shewn thee new things from that time, and things are kept which thou knowest not:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They are created now, and not of old: and before the day, when thou heardest them not, lest thou shouldst say: Behold I knew them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast neither heard, nor known, neither was thy ear opened of old. For I know that transgressing thou wilt transgress, and I have called thee a transgressor from the womb.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For my name’s sake I will remove my wrath far off: and for my praise I will bridle thee, lest thou shouldst perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I have refined thee, but not as silver, I have chosen thee in the furnace of poverty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For my own sake, for my own sake will I do it, that I may not be blasphemed: and I will not give my glory to another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hearken to me, O Jacob, and thou Israel whom I call: I am he, I am the first, and I am the last.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My hand also hath founded the earth, and my right hand hath measured the heavens: I shall call them, and they shall stand together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Assemble yourselves together, all you, and hear: who among them hath declared these things? the Lord hath loved him, he will do his pleasure in Babylon, and his arm shall be on the Chaldeans.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I, even I have spoken and called him: I have brought him, and his way is made prosperous.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Come ye near unto me, and hear this: I have not spoken in secret from the beginning: from the time before it was done, I was there, and now the Lord God hath sent me, and his spirit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord thy redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am the Lord thy God that teach thee profitable things, that govern thee in the way that thou walkest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O that thou hadst hearkened to my commandments: thy peace had been as a river, and thy justice as the waves of the sea,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thy seed had been as the sand, and the offspring of thy bowels like the gravel thereof: his name should not have perished, nor have been destroyed from before my face.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Come forth out of Babylon, flee ye from the Chaldeans, declare it with the voice of joy: make this to be heard, and speak it out even to the ends of the earth. Say: The Lord hath redeemed his servant Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They thirsted not in the desert, when he led them out: he brought forth water out of the rock for them, and he clove the rock, and the waters gushed out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is no peace to the wicked, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give ear, ye islands, and hearken, ye people from afar. The Lord hath called me from the womb, from the bowels of my mother he hath been mindful of my name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he hath made my mouth like a sharp sword: in the shadow of his hand he hath protected me, and hath made me as a chosen arrow: in his quiver he hath hidden me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to me: Thou art my servant Israel, for in thee will I glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I said: I have laboured in vain, I have spent my strength without cause and in vain: therefore my judgment is with the Lord, and my work with my God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now saith the Lord, that formed me from the womb to be his servant, that I may bring back Jacob unto him, and Israel will not be gathered together: and I am glorified in the eyes of the Lord, and my God is made my strength.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: It is a small thing that thou shouldst be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob, and to convert the dregs of Israel. Behold, I have given thee to be the light of the Gentiles, that thou mayst be my salvation even to the farthest part of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord the redeemer of Israel, his Holy One, to the soul that is despised, to the nation that is abhorred, to the servant of rulers: Kings shall see, and princes shall rise up, and adore for the Lord’s sake, because he is faithful, and for the Holy One of Israel, who hath chosen thee.
