source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord: In an acceptable time I have heard thee, and in the day of salvation I have helped thee: and I have preserved thee, and given thee to be a covenant of the people, that thou mightest raise up the earth, and possess the inheritances that were destroyed:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou mightest say to them that are bound: Come forth: and to them that are in darkness: Shew yourselves. They shall feed in the ways, and their pastures shall be in every plain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall not hunger, nor thirst, neither shall the heat nor the sun strike them: for he that is merciful to them, shall be their shepherd, and at the fountains of waters he shall give them drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make all my mountains a way, and my paths shall be exalted.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold these shall come from afar, and behold these from the north and from the sea, and these from the south country.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give praise, O ye heavens, and rejoice, O earth, ye mountains, give praise with jubilation: because the Lord hath comforted his people, and will have mercy on his poor ones.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Sion said: The Lord hath forsaken me, and the Lord hath forgotten me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Can a woman forget her infant, so as not to have pity on the son of her womb? and if she should forget, yet will not I forget thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, I have graven thee in my hands: thy walls are always before my eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy builders are come: they that destroy thee and make thee waste shall go out of thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lift up thy eyes round about, and see all these are gathered together, they are come to thee: I live, saith the Lord, thou shalt be clothed with all these as with an ornament, and as a bride thou shalt put them about thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thy deserts, and thy desolate places, and the land of thy destruction shall now be too narrow by reason of the inhabitants, and they that swallowed thee up shall be chased far away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The children of thy barrenness shall still say in thy ears: The place is too strait for me, make me room to dwell in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt say in thy heart: Who hath begotten these? I was barren and brought not forth, led away, and captive: and who hath brought up these? I was destitute and alone: and these, where were they?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord God: Behold I will lift up my hand to the Gentiles, and will set up my standard to the people. And they shall bring thy sons in their arms, and carry thy daughters upon their shoulders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And kings shall be thy nursing fathers, and queens thy nurses: they shall worship thee with their face toward the earth, and they shall lick up the dust of thy feet. And thou shalt know that I am the Lord, for they shall not be confounded that wait for him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall the prey be taken from the strong? or can that which was taken by the mighty, be delivered?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord: Yea verily, even the captivity shall be taken away from the strong: and that which was taken by the mighty, shall be delivered. But I will judge those that have judged thee, and thy children I will save.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will feed thy enemies with their own flesh: and they shall be made drunk with their own blood, as with new wine: and all flesh shall know, that I am the Lord that save thee, and thy Redeemer the Mighty One of Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord: What is this bill of the divorce of your mother, with which I have put her away? or who is my creditor, to whom I sold you: behold you are sold for your iniquities, and for your wicked deeds have I put your mother away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because I came, and there was not a man: I called, and there was none that would hear. Is my hand shortened and become little, that I cannot redeem? or is there no strength in me to deliver? Behold at my rebuke I will make the sea a desert, I will turn the rivers into dry land: the fishes shall rot for want of water, and shall die for thirst.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will clothe the heavens with darkness, and will make sackcloth their covering.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord hath given me a learned tongue, that I should know how to uphold by word him that is weary: he wakeneth in the morning, in the morning he wakeneth my ear, that I may hear him as a master.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord God hath opened my ear, and I do not resist: I have not gone back.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have given my body to the strikers, and my cheeks to them that plucked them: I have not turned away my face from them that rebuked me, and spit upon me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord God is my helper, therefore am I not confounded: therefore have I set my face as a most hard rock, and I know that I shall not be confounded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He is near that justifieth me, who will contend with me? let us stand together, who is my adversary? let him come near to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the Lord God is my helper: who is he that shall condemn me? Lo, they shall all be destroyed as a garment, the moth shall eat them up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is there among you that feareth the Lord, that heareth the voice of his servant, that hath walked in darkness, and hath no light? let him hope in the name of the Lord, and lean upon his God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold all you that kindle a fire, encompassed with flames, walk in the light of your fire, and in the flames which you have kindled: this is done to you by my hand, you shall sleep in sorrows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give ear to me, you that follow that which is just, and you that seek the Lord: look unto the rock whence you are hewn, and to the hole of the pit from which you are dug out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Look unto Abraham your father, and to Sara that bore you: for I called him alone, and blessed him, and multiplied him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord therefore will comfort Sion, and will comfort all the ruins thereof: and he will make her desert as a place of pleasure, and her wilderness as the garden of the Lord. Joy and gladness shall be found therein, thanksgiving, and the voice of praise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hearken unto me, O my people, and give ear to me, O my tribes: for a law shall go forth from me, and my judgment shall rest to be a light of the nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My just one is near at hand, my saviour is gone forth, and my arms shall judge the people: the islands shall look for me, and shall patiently wait for my arm.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lift up your eyes to heaven, and look down to the earth beneath: for the heavens shall vanish like smoke, and the earth shall be worn away like a garment, and the inhabitants thereof shall perish in like manner: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my justice shall not fail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hearken to me, you that know what is just, my people who have my law in your heart: fear ye not the reproach of men, and be not afraid of their blasphemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the worm shall eat them up as a garment: and the moth shall consume them as wool: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my justice from generation to generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arise, arise, put on strength, O thou arm of the Lord, arise as in the days of old, in the ancient generations. Hast not thou struck the proud one, and wounded the dragon?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hast not thou dried up the sea, the water of the mighty deep, who madest the depth of the sea a way, that the delivered might pass over?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now they that are redeemed by the Lord, shall return, and shall come into Sion singing praises, and joy everlasting shall be upon their heads, they shall obtain joy and gladness, sorrow and mourning shall flee away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I, I myself will comfort you: who art thou, that thou shouldst be afraid of a mortal man, and of the son of man, who shall wither away like grass?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou hast forgotten the Lord thy maker, who stretched out the heavens, and founded the earth: and thou hast been afraid continually all the day at the presence of his fury who afflicted thee, and had prepared himself to destroy thee: where is now the fury of the oppressor?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall quickly come that is going to open unto you, and he shall not kill unto utter destruction, neither shall his bread fail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I am the Lord thy God, who trouble the sea, and the waves thereof swell: the Lord of hosts is my name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have put my words in thy mouth, and have protected thee in the shadow of my hand, that thou mightest plant the heavens, and found the earth: and mightest say to Sion: Thou art my people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arise, arise, stand up, O Jerusalem, which hast drunk at the hand of the Lord the cup of his wrath; thou hast drunk even to the bottom of the cup of dead sleep, and thou hast drunk even to the dregs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is none that can uphold her among all the children that she hath brought forth: and there is none that taketh her by the hand among all the children that she hath brought up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There are two things that have happened to thee: who shall be sorry for thee? desolation, and destruction, and the famine, and the sword, who shall comfort thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy children are cast forth, they have slept at the head of all the ways, as the wild ox that is snared: full of the indignation of the Lord, of the rebuke of thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore hear this, thou poor little one, and thou that art drunk but not with wine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith thy Sovereign the Lord, and thy God, who will fight for his people: Behold I have taken out of thy hand the cup of dead sleep, the dregs of the cup of my indignation, thou shalt not drink it again any more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will put it in the hand of them that have oppressed thee, and have said to thy soul: Bow down, that we may go over: and thou hast laid thy body as the ground, and as a way to them that went over.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arise, arise, put on thy strength, O Sion, put on the garments of thy glory, O Jerusalem, the city of the Holy One: for henceforth the uncircumcised, and unclean shall no more pass through thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shake thyself from the dust, arise, sit up, O Jerusalem: loose the bonds from off thy neck, O captive daughter of Sion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord: You were sold gratis, and you shall be redeemed, without money.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord God: My people went down into Egypt at the beginning to sojourn there: and the Assyrian hath oppressed them without any cause at all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now what have I here, saith the Lord: for my people is taken away gratis. They that rule over them treat them unjustly, saith the Lord, and my name is continually blasphemed all the day long.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore my people shall know my name in that day: for I myself that spoke, behold I am here.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him that bringeth good tidings, and that preacheth peace: of him that sheweth forth good, that preacheth salvation, that saith to Sion: Thy God shall reign!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The voice of thy watchmen: they have lifted up their voice, they shall praise together: for they shall see eye to eye when the Lord shall convert Sion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rejoice, and give praise together, O ye deserts of Jerusalem: for the Lord hath comforted his people: he hath redeemed Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord hath prepared his holy arm in the sight of all the Gentiles: and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of our God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Depart, depart, go ye out from thence, touch no unclean thing: go out of the midst of her, be ye clean, you that carry the vessels of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For you shall not go out in a tumult, neither shall you make haste by flight: for the Lord will go before you, and the God of Israel will gather you together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold my servant shall understand, he shall be exalted, and extolled, and shall be exceeding high.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As many have been astonished at thee, so shall his visage be inglorious among men, and his form among the sons of men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall sprinkle many nations, kings shall shut their mouth at him: for they to whom it was not told of him, have seen: and they that heard not, have beheld.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath believed our report? and to whom is the arm of the Lord revealed?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall grow up as a tender plant before him, and as a root out of a thirsty ground: there is no beauty in him, nor comeliness: and we have seen him, and there was no sightliness, that we should be desirous of him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Despised, and the most abject of men, a man of sorrows, and acquainted with infirmity: and his look was as it were hidden and despised, whereupon we esteemed him not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Surely he hath borne our infirmities and carried our sorrows: and we have thought him as it were a leper, and as one struck by God and afflicted.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he was wounded for our iniquities, he was bruised for our sins: the chastisement of our peace was upon him, and by his bruises we are healed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All we like sheep have gone astray, every one hath turned aside into his own way: and the Lord hath laid on him the iniquity of us all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He was offered because it was his own will, and he opened not his mouth: he shall be led as a sheep to the slaughter, and shall be dumb as a lamb before his shearer, and he shall not open his mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He was taken away from distress, and from judgment: who shall declare his generation? because he is cut off out of the land of the living: for the wickedness of my people have I struck him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall give the ungodly for his burial, and the rich for his death: because he hath done no iniquity, neither was there deceit in his mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord was pleased to bruise him in infirmity: if he shall lay down his life for sin, he shall see a longlived seed, and the will of the Lord shall be prosperous in his hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because his soul hath laboured, he shall see and be filled: by his knowledge shall this my just servant justify many, and he shall bear their iniquities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore will I distribute to him very many, and he shall divide the spoils of the strong, because he hath delivered his soul unto death, and was reputed with the wicked: and he hath borne the sins of many, and hath prayed for the transgressors.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give praise, O thou barren, that bearest not: sing forth praise, and make a joyful noise, thou that didst not travail with child: for many are the children of the desolate, more than of her that hath a husband, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Enlarge the place of thy tent, and stretch out the skins of thy tabernacles, spare not: lengthen thy cords, and strengthen thy stakes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thou shalt pass on to the right hand, and to the left: and thy seed shall inherit the Gentiles, and shall inhabit the desolate cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear not, for thou shalt not be confounded, nor blush: for thou shalt not be put to shame, because thou shalt forget the shame of thy youth, and shalt remember no more the reproach of thy widowhood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he that made thee shall rule over thee, the Lord of hosts is his name: and thy Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel, shall be called the God of all the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord hath called thee as a woman forsaken and mourning in spirit, and as a wife cast off from her youth, said thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For a small moment have I forsaken thee, but with great mercies will I gather thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In a moment of indignation have I hid my face a little while from thee, but with everlasting kindness have I had mercy on thee, said the Lord thy Redeemer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This thing is to me as in the days of Noe, to whom I swore, that I would no more bring in the waters of Noe upon the earth: so have I sworn not to be angry with thee, and not to rebuke thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the mountains shall be moved, and the hills shall tremble; but my mercy shall not depart from thee, and the covenant of my peace shall not be moved: said the Lord that hath mercy on thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O poor little one, tossed with tempest, without all comfort, behold I will lay thy stones in order, and will lay thy foundations with sapphires,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make thy bulwarks of jasper: and thy gates of graven stones, and all thy borders of desirable stones.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All thy children shall be taught of the Lord: and great shall be the peace of thy children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt be founded in justice: depart far from oppression, for thou shalt not fear; and from terror, for it shall not come near thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, an inhabitant shall come, who was not with me, he that was a stranger to thee before, shall be joined to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, I have created the smith that bloweth the coals in the fire, and bringeth forth an instrument for his work, and I have created the killer to destroy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No weapon that is formed against thee shall prosper: and every tongue that resisteth thee in judgment, thou shalt condemn. This is the inheritance of the servants of the Lord, and their justice with me, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All you that thirst, come to the waters: and you that have no money make haste, buy, and eat: come ye, buy wine and milk without money, and without any price.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why do you spend money for that which is not bread, and your labour for that which doth not satisfy you? Hearken diligently to me, and eat that which is good, and your soul shall be delighted in fatness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Incline your ear and come to me: hear and your soul shall live, and I will make an everlasting covenant with you, the faithful mercies of David.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I have given him for a witness to the people, for a leader and a master to the Gentiles.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold thou shalt call a nation, which thou knewest not: and the nations that knew not thee shall run to thee, because of the Lord thy God, and for the Holy One of Israel, for he hath glorified thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seek ye the Lord, while he may be found: call upon him, while he is near.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the wicked forsake his way, and the unjust man his thoughts, and let him return to the Lord, and he will have mercy on him, and to our God: for he is bountiful to forgive.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For my thoughts are not your thoughts: nor your ways my ways, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For as the heavens are exalted above the earth, so are my ways exalted above your ways, and my thoughts above your thoughts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as the rain and the snow come down from heaven, and return no more thither, but soak the earth, and water it, and make it to spring, and give seed to the sower, and bread to the eater:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So shall my word be, which shall go forth from my mouth: it shall not return to me void, but it shall do whatsoever I please, and shall prosper in the things for which I sent it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For you shall go out with joy, and be led forth with peace: the mountains and the hills shall sing praise before you, and all the trees of the country shall clap their hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Instead of the shrub, shall come up the fir tree, and instead of the nettle, shall come up the myrtle tree: and the Lord shall be named for an everlasting sign, that shall not be taken away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord: Keep ye judgment, and do justice: for my salvation is near to come, and my justice to be revealed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed is the man that doth this, and the son of man that shall lay hold on this: that keepeth the sabbath from profaning it, that keepeth his hands from doing any evil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And let not the son of the stranger, that adhereth to the Lord, speak, saying: The Lord will divide and separate me from his people. And let not the eunuch say: Behold I am a dry tree.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord to the eunuchs, They that shall keep my sabbaths, and shall choose the things that please me, and shall hold fast my covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will give to them in my house, and within my walls, a place, and a name better than sons and daughters: I will give them an everlasting name which shall never perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of the stranger that adhere to the Lord, to worship him, and to love his name, to be his servants: every one that keepeth the sabbath from profaning it, and that holdeth fast my covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will bring them into my holy mount, and will make them joyful in my house of prayer: their holocausts, and their victims shall please me upon my altar: for my house shall be called the house of prayer, for all nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord God, who gathereth the scattered of Israel, saith: I will still gather unto him his congregation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All ye beasts of the field come to devour, all ye beasts of the forest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His watchmen are all blind, they are all ignorant: dumb dogs not able to bark, seeing vain things, sleeping and loving dreams.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And most impudent dogs, they never had enough: the shepherds themselves knew no understanding: all have turned aside into their own way, every one after his own gain, from the first even to the last.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Come, let us take wine, and be filled with drunkenness: and it shall be as to day, so also to morrow, and much more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The just perisheth, and no man layeth it to heart, and men of mercy are taken away, because there is none that understandeth; for the just man is taken away from before the face of evil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let peace come, let him rest in his bed that hath walked in his uprightness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But draw near hither, you sons of the sorceress, the seed of the adulterer, and of the harlot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Upon whom have you jested? upon whom have you opened your mouth wide, and put out your tongue? are not you wicked children, a false seed,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who seek your comfort in idols under every green tree, sacrificing children in the torrents, under the high rocks?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the parts of the torrent is thy portion, this is thy lot: and thou hast poured out libations to them, thou hast offered sacrifice. Shall I not be angry at these things?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Upon a high and lofty mountain thou hast laid thy bed, and hast gone up thither to offer victims.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behind the door, and behind the post thou hast set up thy remembrance: for thou hast discovered thyself near me, and hast received an adulterer: thou hast enlarged thy bed, and made a covenant with them: thou hast loved their bed with open hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou hast adorned thyself for the king with ointment, and hast multiplied thy perfumes. Thou hast sent thy messengers far off, and wast debased even to hell.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast been wearied in the multitude of thy ways: yet thou saidst not: I will rest: thou hast found life of thy hand, therefore thou hast not asked.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For whom hast thou been solicitous and afraid, that thou hast lied, and hast not been mindful of me, nor thought on me in thy heart? for I am silent, and as one that seeth not, and thou hast forgotten me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will declare thy justice, and thy works shall not profit thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou shalt cry, let thy companies deliver thee, but the wind shall carry them all off, a breeze shall take them away, but he that putteth his trust in me, shall inherit the land, and shall possess my holy mount.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will say: Make a way: give free passage, turn out of the path, take away the stumblingblocks out of the way of my people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the High and the Eminent that inhabiteth eternity: and his name is Holy, who dwelleth in the high and holy place, and with a contrite and humble spirit, to revive the spirit of the humble, and to revive the heart of the contrite.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I will not contend for ever, neither will I be angry unto the end: because the spirit shall go forth from my face, and breathings I will make.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the iniquity of his covetousness I was angry, and I struck him: I hid my face from thee, and was angry: and he went away wandering in his own heart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I saw his ways, and I healed him, and brought him back, and restored comforts to him, and to them that mourn for him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I created the fruit of the lips, peace, peace to him that is far off, and to him that is near, said the Lord, and I healed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the wicked are like the raging sea, which cannot rest, and the waves thereof cast up dirt and mire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is no peace to the wicked, saith the Lord God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cry, cease not, lift up thy voice like a trumpet, and shew my people their wicked doings, and the house of Jacob their sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they seek me from day to day, and desire to know my ways, as a nation that hath done justice, and hath not forsaken the judgment of their God: they ask of me the judgments of justice: they are willing to approach to God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why have we fasted, and thou hast not regarded: have we humbled our souls, and thou hast not taken notice? Behold in the day of your fast your own will is found, and you exact of all your debtors.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold you fast for debates and strife, and strike with the fist wickedly. Do not fast as you have done until this day, to make your cry to be heard on high.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is this such a fast as I have chosen: for a man to afflict his soul for a day? is this it, to wind his head about like a circle, and to spread sackcloth and ashes? wilt thou call this a fast, and a day acceptable to the Lord?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is not this rather the fast that I have chosen? loose the bands of wickedness, undo the bundles that oppress, let them that are broken go free, and break asunder every burden.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Deal thy bread to the hungry, and bring the needy and the harbourless into thy house: when thou shalt see one naked, cover him, and despise not thy own flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall thy light break forth as the morning, and thy health shall speedily arise, and thy justice shall go before thy face, and the glory of the Lord shall gather thee up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shalt thou call, and the Lord shall hear: thou shalt cry, and he shall say, Here I am. If thou wilt take away the chain out of the midst of thee, and cease to stretch out the finger, and to speak that which profiteth not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou shalt pour out thy soul to the hungry, and shalt satisfy the afflicted soul, then shall thy light rise up in darkness, and thy darkness shall be as the noonday.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord will give thee rest continually, and will fill thy soul with brightness, and deliver thy bones, and thou shalt be like a watered garden, and like a fountain of water whose waters shall not fail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the places that have been desolate for ages shall be built in thee: thou shalt raise up the foundations of generation and generation: and thou shalt be called the repairer of the fences, turning the paths into rest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou turn away thy foot from the sabbath, from doing thy own will in my holy day, and call the sabbath delightful, and the holy of the Lord glorious, and glorify him, while thou dost not thy own ways, and thy own will is not found, to speak a word:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shalt thou be delighted in the Lord, and I will lift thee up above the high places of the earth, and will feed thee with the inheritance of Jacob thy father. For the mouth of the Lord hath spoken it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the hand of the Lord is not shortened that it cannot save, neither is his ear heavy that it cannot hear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But your iniquities have divided between you and your God, and your sins have hid his face from you that he should not hear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For your hands are defiled with blood, and your fingers with iniquity: your lips have spoken lies, and your tongue uttereth iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is none that calleth upon justice, neither is there any one that judgeth truly: but they trust in a mere nothing, and speak vanities: they have conceived labour, and brought forth iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have broken the eggs of asps, and have woven the webs of spiders: he that shall eat of their eggs, shall die: and that which is brought out, shall be hatched into a basilisk.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their webs shall not be for clothing, neither shall they cover themselves with their works: their works are unprofitable works, and the work of iniquity is in their hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed innocent blood: their thoughts are unprofitable thoughts: wasting and destruction are in their ways.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have not known the way of peace, and there is no judgment in their steps: their paths are become crooked to them, every one that treadeth in them knoweth no peace.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore is judgment far from us, and justice shall not overtake us. We looked for light, and behold darkness: brightness, and we have walked in the dark.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We have groped for the wall, and like the blind we have groped as if we had no eyes: we have stumbled at noonday as in darkness, we are in dark places, as dead men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We shall roar all of us like bears, and shall lament as mournful doves. We have looked for judgment, and there is none: for salvation, and it is far from us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For our iniquities are multiplied before thee, and our sins have testified against us: for our wicked doings are with us, and we have known our iniquities:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In sinning and lying against the Lord: and we have turned away so that we went not after our God, but spoke calumny and transgression: we have conceived, and uttered from the heart, words of falsehood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And judgment is turned away backward, and justice hath stood far off: because truth hath fallen down in the street, and equity could not come in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And truth hath been forgotten: and he that departed from evil, lay open to be a prey: and the Lord saw, and it appeared evil in his eyes, because there is no judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he saw that there is not a man: and he stood astonished, because there is none to oppose himself: and his own arm brought salvation to him, and his own justice supported him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He put on justice as a breastplate, and a helmet of salvation upon his head: he put on the garments of vengeance, and was clad with zeal as with a cloak.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As unto revenge, as it were to repay wrath to his adversaries, and a reward to his enemies: he will repay the like to the islands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they from the west, shall fear the name of the Lord: and they from the rising of the sun, his glory when he shall come as a violent stream, which the spirit of the Lord driveth on:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall come a redeemer to Sion, and to them that return from iniquity in Jacob, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is my covenant with them, saith the Lord: My spirit that is in thee, and my words that I have put in thy mouth, shall not depart out of thy mouth, nor out of the mouth of thy seed, nor out of the mouth of thy seed’s seed, saith the Lord, from henceforth and for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arise, be enlightened, O Jerusalem: for thy light is come, and the glory of the Lord is risen upon thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For behold darkness shall cover the earth, and a mist the people: but the Lord shall arise upon thee, and his glory shall be seen upon thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Gentiles shall walk in thy light, and kings in the brightness of thy rising.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lift up thy eyes round about, and see: all these are gathered together, they are come to thee: thy sons shall come from afar, and thy daughters shall rise up at thy side.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shalt thou see, and abound, and thy heart shall wonder and be enlarged, when the multitude of the sea shall be converted to thee, the strength of the Gentiles shall come to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The multitude of camels shall cover thee, the dromedaries of Madian and Epha: all they from Saba shall come, bringing gold and frankincense: and shewing forth praise to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the flocks of Cedar shall be gathered together unto thee, the rams of Nabaioth shall minister to thee: they shall be offered upon my acceptable altar, and I will glorify the house of my majesty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who are these, that fly as clouds, and as doves to their windows?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For, the islands wait for me, and the ships of the sea in the beginning: that I may bring thy sons from afar: their silver, and their gold with them, to the name of the Lord thy God, and to the Holy One of Israel, because he hath glorified thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of strangers shall build up thy walls, and their kings shall minister to thee: for in my wrath have I struck thee, and in my reconciliation have I had mercy upon thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thy gates shall be open continually: they shall not be shut day nor night, that the strength of the Gentiles may be brought to thee, and their kings may be brought.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the nation and the kingdom that will not serve thee, shall perish: and the Gentiles shall be wasted with desolation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The glory of Libanus shall come to thee, the fir tree, and the box tree, and the pine tree together, to beautify the place of my sanctuary: and I will glorify the place of my feet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of them that afflict thee, shall come bowing down to thee, and all that slandered thee shall worship the steps of thy feet, and shall call thee the city of the Lord, the Sion of the Holy One of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because thou wast forsaken, and hated, and there was none that passed through thee, I will make thee to be an everlasting glory, a joy unto generation and generation:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt suck the milk of the Gentiles, and thou shalt be nursed with the breasts of kings: and thou shalt know that I am the Lord thy Saviour, and thy Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For brass I will bring gold, and for iron I will bring silver: and for wood brass, and for stones iron: and I will make thy visitation peace, and thy overseers justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Iniquity shall no more be heard in thy land, wasting nor destruction in thy borders, and salvation shall possess thy walls, and praise thy gates.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt no more have the sun for thy light by day, neither shall the brightness of the moon enlighten thee: but the Lord shall be unto thee for an everlasting light, and thy God for thy glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy sun shall go down no more, and thy moon shall not decrease: for the Lord shall be unto thee for an everlasting light, and the days of thy mourning shall be ended.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thy people shall be all just, they shall inherit the land for ever, the branch of my planting, the work of my hand to glorify me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The least shall become a thousand, and a little one a most strong nation: I the Lord will suddenly do this thing in its time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord hath anointed me: he hath sent me to preach to the meek, to heal the contrite of heart, and to preach a release to the captives, and deliverance to them that are shut up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord, and the day of vengeance of our God: to comfort all that mourn:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To appoint to the mourners of Sion, and to give them a crown for ashes, the oil of joy for mourning, a garment of praise for the spirit of grief: and they shall be called in it the mighty ones of justice, the planting of the Lord to glorify him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall build the places that have been waste from of old, and shall raise up ancient ruins, and shall repair the desolate cities, that were destroyed for generation and generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And strangers shall stand and shall feed your flocks: and the sons of strangers shall be your husbandmen, and the dressers of your vines.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you shall be called the priests of the Lord: to you it shall be said: Ye ministers of our God: you shall eat the riches of the Gentiles, and you shall pride yourselves in their glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For your double confusion and shame, they shall praise their part: therefore shall they receive double in their land, everlasting joy shall be unto them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I am the Lord that love judgment, and hate robbery in a holocaust: and I will make their work in truth, and I will make a perpetual covenant with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall know their seed among the Gentiles, and their offspring in the midst of peoples: all that shall see them, shall know them, that these are the seed which the Lord hath blessed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will greatly rejoice in the Lord, and my soul shall be joyful in my God: for he hath clothed me with the garments of salvation: and with the robe of justice he hath covered me, as a bridegroom decked with a crown, and as a bride adorned with her jewels.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For as the earth bringeth forth her bud, and as the garden causeth her seed to shoot forth: so shall the Lord God make justice to spring forth, and praise before all the nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For Sion’s sake I will not hold my peace, and for the sake of Jerusalem, I will not rest till her just one come forth as brightness, and her saviour be lighted as a lamp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Gentiles shall see thy just one, and all kings thy glorious one: and thou shalt be called by a new name, which the mouth of the Lord shall name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt be a crown of glory in the hand of the Lord, and a royal diadem in the hand of thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt no more be called Forsaken: and thy land shall no more be called Desolate: but thou shalt be called My pleasure in her, and thy land inhabited. Because the Lord hath been well pleased with thee: and thy land shall be inhabited.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the young man shall dwell with the virgin, and thy children shall dwell in thee. And the bridegroom shall rejoice over the bride, and thy God shall rejoice over thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Upon thy walls, O Jerusalem, I have appointed watchmen all the day, and all the night, they shall never hold their peace. You that are mindful of the Lord, hold not your peace,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And give him no silence till he establish, and till he make Jerusalem a praise in the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord hath sworn by his right hand, and by the arm of his strength: Surely I will no more give thy corn to be meat for thy enemies: and the sons of the strangers shall not drink thy wine, for which thou hast laboured.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they that gather it, shall eat it, and shall praise the Lord: and they that bring it together, shall drink it in my holy courts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go through, go through the gates, prepare the way for the people, make the road plain, pick out the stones, and lift up the standard to the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the Lord hath made it to be heard in the ends of the earth, tell the daughter of Sion: Behold thy Saviour cometh: behold his reward is with him, and his work before him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall call them, The holy people, the redeemed of the Lord. But thou shalt be called: A city sought after, and not forsaken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is this that cometh from Edom, with dyed garments from Bosra, this beautiful one in his robe, walking in the greatness of his strength. I, that speak justice, and am a defender to save.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why then is thy apparel red, and thy garments like theirs that tread in the winepress?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have trodden the winepress alone, and of the Gentiles there is not a man with me: I have trampled on them in my indignation, and have trodden them down in my wrath, and their blood is sprinkled upon my garments, and I have stained all my apparel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the day of vengeance is in my heart, the year of my redemption is come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I looked about, and there was none to help: I sought, and there was none to give aid: and my own arm hath saved for me, and my indignation itself hath helped me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I have trodden down the people in my wrath, and have made them drunk in my indignation, and have brought down their strength to the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will remember the tender mercies of the Lord, the praise of the Lord for all the things that the Lord hath bestowed upon us, and for the multitude of his good things to the house of Israel, which he hath given them according to his kindness, and according to the multitude of his mercies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Surely they are my people, children that will not deny: so he became their saviour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In all their affliction he was not troubled, and the angel of his presence saved them: in his love, and in his mercy he redeemed them, and he carried them and lifted them up all the days of old.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they provoked to wrath, and afflicted the spirit of his Holy One: and he was turned to be their enemy, and he fought against them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he remembered the days of old of Moses, and of his people: Where is he that brought them up out of the sea, with the shepherds of his flock? where is he that put in the midst of them the spirit of his Holy One?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that brought out Moses by the right hand, by the arm of his majesty: that divided the waters before them, to make himself an everlasting name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that led them out through the deep, as a horse in the wilderness that stumbleth not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As a beast that goeth down in the field, the spirit of the Lord was their leader: so didst thou lead thy people to make thyself a glorious name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Look down from heaven, and behold from thy holy habitation and the place of thy glory: where is thy zeal, and thy strength, the multitude of thy bowels, and of thy mercies? they have held back themselves from me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thou art our father, and Abraham hath not known us, and Israel hath been ignorant of us: thou, O Lord, art our father, our redeemer, from everlasting is thy name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why hast thou made us to err, O Lord, from thy ways: why hast thou hardened our heart, that we should not fear thee? return for the sake of thy servants, the tribes of thy inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have possessed thy holy people as nothing: our enemies have trodden down thy sanctuary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We are become as in the beginning, when thou didst not rule over us, and when we were not called by thy name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O that thou wouldst rend the heavens, and wouldst come down: the mountains would melt away at thy presence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They would melt as at the burning of fire, the waters would burn with fire, that thy name might be made known to thy enemies: that the nations might tremble at thy presence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou shalt do wonderful things, we shall not bear them: thou didst come down, and at thy presence the mountains melted away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From the beginning of the world they have not heard, nor perceived with the ears: the eye hath not seen, O God, besides thee, what things thou hast prepared for them that wait for thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast met him that rejoiceth, and doth justice: in thy ways they shall remember thee: behold thou art angry, and we have sinned: in them we have been always, and we shall be saved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we are all become as one unclean, and all our justices as the rag of a menstruous woman: and we have all fallen as a leaf, and our iniquities, like the wind, have taken us away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is none that calleth upon thy name: that riseth up, and taketh hold of thee: thou hast hid thy face from us, and hast crushed us in the hand of our iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now, O Lord, thou art our father, and we are clay: and thou art our maker, and we all are the works of thy hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be not very angry, O Lord, and remember no longer our iniquity: behold, see we are all thy people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The city of thy sanctuary is become a desert, Sion is made a desert, Jerusalem is desolate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The house of our holiness, and of our glory, where our fathers praised thee, is burnt with fire, and all our lovely things are turned into ruins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wilt thou refrain thyself, O Lord, upon these things, wilt thou hold thy peace, and afflict us vehemently?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have sought me that before asked not for me, they have found me that sought me not. I said: Behold me, behold me, to a nation that did not call upon my name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have spread forth my hands all the day to an unbelieving people, who walk in a way that is not good after their own thoughts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A people that continually provoke me to anger before my face, that immolate in gardens, and sacrifice upon bricks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That dwell in sepulchres, and sleep in the temple of idols: that eat swine’s flesh, and profane broth is in their vessels.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That say: Depart from me, come not near me, because thou art unclean: these shall be smoke in my anger, a fire burning all the day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold it is written before me: I will not be silent, but I will render and repay into their bosom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your iniquities, and the iniquities of your fathers together, saith the Lord, who have sacrificed upon the mountains, and have reproached me upon the hills; and I will measure back their first work in their bosom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord: As if a grain be found in a cluster, and it be said: Destroy it not, because it is a blessing: so will I do for the sake of my servants, that I may not destroy the whole.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will bring forth a seed out of Jacob, and out of Juda a possessor of my mountains: and my elect shall inherit it, and my servants shall dwell there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the plains shall be turned to folds of flocks, and the valley of Achor into a place for the herds to lie down in, for my people that have sought me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you, that have forsaken the Lord, that have forgotten my holy mount, that set a table for fortune, and offer libations upon it,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will number you in the sword, and you shall all fall by slaughter: because I called and you did not answer: I spoke, and you did not hear: and you did evil in my eyes, and you have chosen the things that displease me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore thus saith the Lord God: Behold my servants shall eat, and you shall be hungry: behold my servants shall drink, and you shall be thirsty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold my servants shall rejoice, and you shall be confounded: behold my servants shall praise for joyfulness of heart, and you shall cry for sorrow of heart, and shall howl for grief of spirit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall leave your name for an execration to my elect: and the Lord God shall slay thee, and call his servants by another name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In which he that is blessed upon the earth, shall be blessed in God, amen: and he that sweareth in the earth, shall swear by God, amen: because the former distresses are forgotten, and because they are hid from my eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For behold I create new heavens, and a new earth: and the former things shall not be in remembrance, and they shall not come upon the heart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you shall be glad and rejoice for ever in these things, which I create: for behold I create Jerusalem a rejoicing, and the people thereof joy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will rejoice in Jerusalem, and joy in my people, and the voice of weeping shall no more be heard in her, nor the voice of crying.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There shall no more be an infant of days there, nor an old man that shall not fill up his days: for the child shall die a hundred years old, and the sinner being a hundred years old shall be accursed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall build houses, and inhabit them; and they shall plant vineyards, and eat the fruits of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall not build, and another inhabit; they shall not plant, and another eat: for as the days of a tree, so shall be the days of my people, and the works of their hands shall be of long continuance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My elect shall not labour in vain, nor bring forth in trouble; for they are the seed of the blessed of the Lord, and their posterity with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass, that before they call, I will hear; as they are yet speaking, I will hear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The wolf and the lamb shall feed together; the lion and the ox shall eat straw; and dust shall be the serpent’s food: they shall not hurt nor kill in all my holy mountain, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord: Heaven is my throne, and the earth my footstool: what is this house that you will build to me? and what is this place of my rest?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My hand made all these things, and all these things were made, saith the Lord. But to whom shall I have respect, but to him that is poor and little, and of a contrite spirit, and that trembleth at my words?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that sacrificeth an ox, is as if he slew a man: he that killeth a sheep in sacrifice, as if he should brain a dog: he that offereth an oblation, as if he should offer swine’s blood; he that remembereth incense, as if he should bless an idol. All these things have they chosen in their ways, and their soul is delighted in their abominations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore I also will choose their mockeries, and will bring upon them the things they feared: because I called, and there was none that would answer; I have spoken, and they heard not; and they have done evil in my eyes, and have chosen the things that displease me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear the word of the Lord, you that tremble at his word: Your brethren that hate you, and cast you out for my name’s sake, have said: Let the Lord be glorified, and we shall see in your joy: but they shall be confounded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A voice of the people from the city, a voice from the temple, the voice of the Lord that rendereth recompense to his enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Before she was in labour, she brought forth; before her time came to be delivered, she brought forth a man child.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath ever heard such a thing? and who hath seen the like to this? shall the earth bring forth in one day? or shall a nation be brought forth at once, because Sion hath been in labour, and hath brought forth her children?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall not I that make others to bring forth children, myself bring forth, saith the Lord? shall I, that give generation to others, be barren, saith the Lord thy God?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rejoice with Jerusalem, and be glad with her, all you that love her: rejoice for joy with her, all you that mourn for her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That you may suck, and be filled with the breasts of her consolations: that you may milk out, and flow with delights, from the abundance of her glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thus saith the Lord: Behold I will bring upon her as it were a river of peace, and as an overflowing torrent the glory of the Gentiles, which you shall suck; you shall be carried at the breasts, and upon the knees they shall caress you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As one whom the mother caresseth, so will I comfort you, and you shall be comforted in Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall see and your heart shall rejoice, and your bones shall flourish like an herb, and the hand of the Lord shall be known to his servants, and he shall be angry with his enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For behold the Lord will come with fire, and his chariots are like a whirlwind, to render his wrath in indignation, and his rebuke with flames of fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord shall judge by fire, and by his sword unto all flesh, and the slain of the Lord shall be many.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that were sanctified, and thought themselves clean in the gardens behind the gate within, they that did eat swine’s flesh, and the abomination, and the mouse: they shall be consumed together, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I know their works, and their thoughts: I come that I may gather them together with all nations and tongues: and they shall come and shall see my glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will set a sign among them, and I will send of them that shall be saved, to the Gentiles into the sea, into Africa, and Lydia them that draw the bow: into Italy, and Greece, to the islands afar off, to them that have not heard of me, and have not seen my glory. And they shall declare my glory to the Gentiles:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall bring all your brethren out of all nations for a gift to the Lord, upon horses, and in chariots, and in litters, and on mules, and in coaches, to my holy mountain Jerusalem, saith the Lord, as if the children of Israel should bring an offering in a clean vessel into the house of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will take of them to be priests, and Levites, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For as the new heavens, and the new earth, which I will make to stand before me, saith the Lord: so shall your seed stand, and your name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be month after month, and sabbath after sabbath: and all flesh shall come to adore before my face, saith the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall go out, and see the carcasses of the men that have transgressed against me: their worm shall not die, and their fire shall not be quenched: and they shall be a loathsome sight to all flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
